E: But do not blame Talanton for their coldness. Go see the broken spine of the land, for that is the price to pay for the endless propagation of life.

C: 例如那弑君者的故事，近卫将军于无月之夜刺杀残暴的君王，却也在事成之后突发暴毙身亡。
E: Take the tale of the kingslayer, the guard captain who assassinated a tyrant king on a moonless night, only to drop dead himself when the deed was done.

C: 如此不通人情的裁决，招来了反对的声浪。民众涌进塔兰顿的神殿，为弑君者奔走呐喊。
E: This merciless verdict sparked outrage. Crowds flooded Talanton's temples, shouting demands for the kingslayer's vindication.

C: 它就像一台精密的机器。
E: They are like an intricate machine.

C: 他们看见一片金色的麦田，鼹鼠钻进土壤，令地面变得松软且适宜耕种。
E: They saw a golden wheat field where the moles would burrow into the soil, making the ground soft and suitable for farming.

C: 听闻如此悲伤的过往，后来的人子们在揣度爱意之时，便会拾起一朵落红。
E: Hearing of such a sorrowful past, the later children of humanity would pick up a fallen red blossom when contemplating love.

C: 人忘却了敬畏，罪有应得。
E: Humanity forgot reverence and deserved punishment.

C: 听到这话的法吉娜也不恼怒，她化身一条巨鲸来到海边，告诉众人，七天之内，如果他们能横渡大海，到达海洋那头的小岛，自己就不再侵扰他们的休憩，还会把自己的宝物赠给他们。
E: Phagousa was not angered by his outburst. Taking the form of a great whale, they appeared by the shore and spoke to the people. If the people could cross the sea and reach a distant island within seven days, they would cease their disturbances and even bestow upon them Phagousa's treasures.

C: 死者的骸骨和冥河的水相结合，被怨声重塑为巨像，它在大地之下找到了艾格勒创造巨人时投下的那把雷枪，用冥河的水淬洗之后，制作成手中的天谴之矛。
E: The bones of the dead, fused with the waters of the River of Souls, were shaped by grief into a colossal figure. Beneath the earth, they unearthed the lightning spear that Aquila had once cast down when creating the giant. Tempered in the waters of the River of Souls, the weapon was reforged into the Lance of Fury.

C: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。你敢肯定自己看见了一轮红日坠入雪山，穿着长袍的少女倒在剑戟之下。甚至，你还听见了些微笑声，真真切切，并非幻想……
E: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. You are sure you saw a red sun descending into the snowy mountains, and a girl in a robe falling under the tip of a sword. You have even heard someone softly laughing. Everything is real, and it is not your imagination...

C: 「又一个系统月过去了。」他叹气，「你说，我们什么时候才能回去？」
E: "Another system month has passed." He sighs. "When do you think we can go back?"

C: 「我们的一切所见、所闻、所思、所想…一切的一切，全都存在于这个*黯点*上——一粒悬浮在射线暴与恒星光中的微尘，一个发着绿色微光的黯点！
E: "Everything we see, hear, think, and feel exists on this DARK SPOT — a speck of dust floating in ray bursts and the sparkle of stars, a dark spot that radiates a feeble green glow!"

C: 吓到你了？抱歉抱歉。
E: Did I scare you? Sorry.

C: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
E: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

C: 终于！踏上久违的大地，意识到终点已至，你不禁露出满足的笑容——尽管这笑容因僵死的面部肌肉未能落到实处。接下来，只要找到那该死的碑铭…！
E: Finally! Stepping on the long-awaited ground, you realize you have reached the endpoint. A smile of satisfaction crawls up your face without you realizing it, though it is not as wide as you want it to be because of your stiff facial muscles. Now, you just need to look for the darn stele...!

C: 「全频带阻塞，和七天前一样，」书呆子的声音依旧不带感情，「而且，没有在任何居住区——不，是任何区域——检测到生体热量排放。」
E: "All frequency bands are blocked, just like a week ago." The nerd speaks in his usual flat tone. "Also, no heat emission of living organisms are detected in the residential areas — or rather, anywhere."

C: 我只知道它、它们就混在我们中间…而且，已经有好几个邻居遭殃了！
E: I just know that th—they're among us... and they've gotten their hands on some of the residents!

C: …不，不，不。我怎么知道你不是那玩意？！别过来！
E: ...No, no. How do I know you're not one of them!? Get away!

C: 接受挑战吧——再巍峨的高山，也敌不过我的全力一击喀！
E: Get ready for a challenge: No matter how great a mountain, there's no way it can take my all-out assault!

C: 真伤脑筋…
E: How vexing...

C: 噹！
E: Dong!

C: 这…兄弟你看，这是——
E: This is... bro, check it out. This is —

C: 三…二…一……
E: Three... Two... One...

C: 很满意
E: Very satisfied.

C: 那我们就一起在这里等其他人到齐吧！
E: Let's wait together here for the others to arrive!

C: …可惜，这里什么都没有。你感到兴味索然，便兀自找了个堆满板条箱的墙角蹲了下来，等待虎克大人前来将你逮捕。
E: ...Unfortunately, you find none of that. You feel disappointed and instead squat on a pile of crates in a corner, waiting for Boss Hook to catch you.

C: 但无论如何，你让我欣赏了一场精彩的演出，我非得给你点回报才行。
E: Anyway. Thanks to you, I get to enjoy an awesome performance. I've got to give you something in return.

C: 你也是希露瓦大人的忠诚仆人，前来瞻仰她的盛世美颜，对吗？
E: You are a loyal servant of Lady Serval. You've come to admire her radiant beauty, right?

C: 那么…就拜托你了！
E: So... I'll leave that to your capable hands!

C: 伤害？怎么可能呢！虽然早就和家里断绝来往了，可我毕竟也是贝洛伯格有头有脸的公众人物，没人敢对我动手动脚的。
E: Hurt me? Impossible! I cut myself off from my family, but I'm still a prominent public figure in Belobog. No one would dare lay a finger on me.

C: 你拍到希露瓦大人的…玉照了吗？！
E: You took Lady Serval's... picture!?

C: 我只是一只虫，为什么要爬到这种地方来…我又没有翅膀喀。
E: I'm just a bug. Why'd I have to crawl all the way up to a place like this? It's not like I even have any wings...

C: 别、别踩虫，虫子也是生命……
E: No... no stepping on bugs. Bugs are living beings, too...

C: 不，不对…似乎不会再有期末测试了喀？
E: No, wait... are there any more final exams?

C: 嗨！朋友，来玩啊~
E: Hi there! Come join me~

C: 他的笑容十分僵硬。
E: His smile is stiff as a board.

C: 你是不是说过同样的台词…
E: Have you said something like that before...

C: 但那可是独眼老奥的家呀！你这是擅闯民宅！
E: This is where Old One-Eyed Ollie lives! You've gone and barged into someone's home!

C: 还是走吧。
E: Maybe next time.
Four...

C: …嘛咪嘛咪哄！好了，我已经帮你拨开了帷幕，快去见识见识吧。
E: ...Eeny, meeny, miny, moe! Alright, I've pulled back the curtain for you. Go and take a look.

C: 我记得那只若虫唱过一段旋律…好像不是这个顺序？
E: I remember this nymph once sang a melody... though it doesn't seem to be in this order?

C: 乌云像山峰一样倾倒，狂风要把长帆撕破。四周只有无边无际的黑暗，你感觉整个世界都把你抛弃了，简直不知道往何处去……
E: The storm clouds topple like mountains, the raging winds threaten to tear the sails apart. Nothing but boundless darkness surrounds you, and it feels as if the whole world has abandoned you. You have no idea where to go...

C: 你尝试解释了你的遭遇，以及你是如何劝说那只生无可恋的机巧鸟回到鹤运物流。
E: You attempted to explain what you encountered as well as how you convinced this cycrane that had nothing left to live for to return to Heron Express.

C: 没有人会对自己的老板敞开心扉。
E: No one would be so open with their own boss.

C: 不打赌多没意思，这样吧，我们再来打一次赌，这次是和这位{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}。
E: It's boring unless we bet... I know, let's place another bet. This time I'll play with this {F#girl}{M#boy} here.

C: 到现在我也没搞明白自己哪里出了纰漏。
E: I still don't understand where I went wrong.

C: 因为看不清命运的形状，所以我才喜欢与人打赌。
E: I like betting with people precisely because I can't see the shape of fate.

C: 没错，万一将来有一天它火了，就是货全老哥赚了。
E: That's right. Should it go viral, then Salesby only stands to make a fortune.

C: …不过，这箱子比外表看上去更重。越是想要行动缓慢不发出声响，身体越是容易出现疲惫的态势，你的力量即将支撑不住。该死，箱子里到底装着什么！
E: ...However, this chest is heavier than it looks. The more effort you put into dampening the sounds of movement, the more exhausted you become — and you'll soon lose your grip. Darn it, what is in this chest!?

C: 你可真是个好人，这个礼物是我自己的，你就收下吧。再见啦！
E: You really are a well-meaning person. Here's a gift from yours truly, keep it. Farewell!

C: 你搞错了，罗浮仙舟还是景元老大的地盘，这一小块地儿是我们的地盘。
E: You've got it wrong. The Xianzhou Luofu still belongs to Jing Yuan. We only own this small piece of land.

C: 站住！我们这里岂是想走就走，想来就来的。江湖上的规矩你不明白吗？
E: Stay right where you are! This is not a place where you can come and go as you please. Don't you know about the unspoken rules of the land?

C: 答案是——「随你便」！
E: The answer to that question is: Whatever you like!

C: …吃到最后不省人事。
E: ...Eating until you pass out.

C: 奖励真值得你用一个撑得硕大的胃袋来换吗？做出了选择的你恐怕心里已经有数了。
E: Is the prize really worth the pain of a distended belly? I'm afraid you've already made your decision — and you know it, too.

C: 不过，大家也觉得不能就这样好像什么也没有发生过…归根结底，就是想问问那个人，为什么要给物流机巧鸟加上智能。
E: But everyone still felt that they couldn't just act as if nothing had happened... In the final analysis, we just wanted to ask their designer why courier cycranes had been imbued with the gift of intelligence.

C: 有点晕，有点想吐…
E: A little nauseated. I think I'm gonna hurl...

C: 这都是什么乱七八糟的。
E: What is this nonsense.

C: 能看着它就这么不声不响地沉进水里，充其量再冒那么几个泡泡意思一下，这对你而言已经是最好的结局了，不是吗？
E: The best outcome for you would be to watch it silently sink into the water, and maybe see a few bubbles rise up at best. Isn't that enough?

C: 当然，我也不是什么懦夫，我和他吵了几天，感觉也没什么意思，就干脆约他出来，咱们当面论道论道。
E: I'm not a coward either. We argued for a few days, but it didn't seem to lead anywhere, so I suggested we meet in person and have a face-to-face discussion.

C: 影城内设的店铺，出售纪念品和小零食。
E: There's a shop inside the theme park selling memorabilia and snacks.

C: 广告牌先生还记得刚开张的那天，花瓣撒满街头，美梦化成的鸟儿在巷子里歌唱。
E: Mr. Billboard still remembers the day he was posted... Petals were scattered across the streets, and birds woven from dreams sang their songs throughout the alleyways.

C: 文学家认为，水管日复一日的哭泣，或许是在哀悼因战争而逝去的恋人。
E: Literature scholars believe that this water pipe cries day after day, perhaps in mourning for a lover lost in war.

C: 我走向它，为它诵念古代无机生命的情诗，于是它也是羞涩地来到我身边，一把抱住我。
E: As I walked towards it, I began to read an ancient inorganic being love poem to it. Hearing this, it shyly approached me and grabbed me close.

C: 从生理角度看，跨越危机才能抵达的美景，会为他们提供更多的正反馈。
E: From a physiological point of view, a beautiful end that they must traverse a crisis to reach is more likely to provide them with more positive emotional feedback.

C: 总算不用打工了！什么打卡什么绩效，全都滚吧！
E: I finally don't have to work anymore! Clocking in? Performance assessments? You can all do one!

C: 没意思，走了。
E: Boring. I'm leaving.

C: 太小孩子气了。
E: This is so childish.

C: 中间的无字碑属于美梦的奠基人「钟表匠」。匠人的指针终将垂降，永远落在指向前方的十二点。
E: The nameless stele in the middle belongs to the founder of dreams: The Watchmaker. The maker's needle finally counted down, pointing forever toward the path forward at twelve o'clock.

C: 你尴尬地顺着椅子扶手滑了下去。
E: You awkwardly slide down the armrest of the chair.

C: 要相信瑟希斯…
E: Believe in Cerces...

C: 我就不打扰你们了。
E: I won't bother you lot.

C: 兄弟，你看着又壮了一圈。
E: Bro, you look stronger than before.

C: 那么大，你不害怕吗…
E: It's huge. Aren't you afraid...?

C: 「搬过去」是什么意思啊？
E: What does "moving over" mean?

C: 刻法勒是我们的敌人吗？
E: Is Kephale our enemy?

C: 便注定要在人世间灭亡。
E: One must inevitably perish in the mortal realm.

C: 再创世前，求积点德吧。
E: Do some good before the Era Nova.

C: 议会广场有人滞留…
E: There are still people trapped in the Council Square...

C: 绝不会松开彼此的手。
E: We'll never let go of each other's hands.

C: 再创世的起始……
E: The dawn of Era Nova...

C: 屏息凝神，来，回想一下三月七是如何拉弓射箭的。投掷与射击，在瞄准的感觉上也许大体一致。
E: Hold your breath and focus. Come on! Remember how March 7th draws and shoots her bow. Throwing, shooting — they both rely on accurate aim.

C: 一起逃？太好了，正好将你们一网打尽。
E: Fleeing together, are you? Great, two birds with one stone then.

C: 我也没给你打一分啊…
E: I didn't give you a score of one though...

C: 哎，我早就试过了，没什么用……
E: Ugh, Pom-Pom tried but it's no use...

C: 我帮你找找吧？
E: Want a hand looking for that?

C: 列车长没骗你吧，是不是很有意思？
E: I wasn't kidding, was I? Isn't it interesting?

C: 那是什么，你刚编的？
E: What's that? Did you just start drafting that now?

C: 那不是她的空间站。
E: That's not Asta's space station.

C: 哎，是帕姆不好，一个不注意就…来，{NICKNAME}乘客，麻烦搭把手！
E: Alas, I can only blame myself for this mess. {NICKNAME}, please give me a hand!

C: 啦啦啦～
E: La, la, la!

C: 变得更加憨态可掬了。
E: You look friendlier and more approachable.

C: 关于「资源补给协议」…
E: About the resource supply agreement...

C: 后来嘛…有些人员变化——邓恩的事你是知道的。再还有贝斯手这些老大难的问题……
E: Then... There were some personnel changes, you know with Dunn... Then there's the perennial problem of our bass player...

C: 感谢你帮助铁卫精进作战技艺——希望你能再来参加后续的演练，{NICKNAME}教官。
E: Thanks for helping the Guards hone our combat skills — We hope you can come back and train with us again sometime, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

C: 这种规格的禁区演练，那小子可撑不住场面啊！
E: That punk can't handle drills of this scale in the restricted zone!

C: 威廉应该跟你提过「照雪灯炉」了，对吧？技术部之前给我科普了那玩意的用法。我凭记忆再给你复述一次，听好了。
E: William must have told you about the Snowshine Lamp, right? The Technology Division has taught me how to use these things before. I'll try and lay it out for you from memory, so listen up.

C: *电波滤音*希望你已经注意到了它逃走的方向，千万不要让它再逃走了!
E: *electronic voice* Make sure you keep an eye on which direction it's heading and don't let it get away again!

C: 您这话说得就见外了。您是大英雄，怎么能亏待呢？
E: Don't mention it. You're the great hero and you deserve it.

C: 你输给了石头老板的爪牙，但不要憎恨你的敌人……
E: Don't hate the enemies that have beaten you, as they are merely Boss Stone's minions...

C: 说你弱你就弱，哼！
E: Because you do look pathetic!

C: 我要出——布！
E: Paper!

C: 这个目中无人的小子已经高高举起他的右手，正准备如他自己所言那般…「会会你」。
E: The haughty kid raises his right hand, wanting a piece of you.

C: 什么什么声音？我，米歇尔，正和你说话呢！对，就在你脚下！
E: Wh—What noise? It's me, Michel, speaking to you right now! Yes, right below you!

C: 你得想办法改变自己消极的人生观了，伙计。不，那才不是人的脚步声，那是——
E: You've got to change your nihilistic views on life, pal. No, that's not the sound of human footsteps. That's...

C: 清点过后，你发现这通缉令统共七张——不过很可惜，即使你费力将这七章画像集齐，也没有被封印的大守护者突然现身，声称能够满足你的任何愿望。
E: You count and find seven Wanted posters in total — but unfortunately, despite collecting all seven of these, no Supreme Guardian pops out of nowhere to grant fulfillment of your wishes.

C: 继续向东。
E: Go further east.

C: 内陆的东北方向是一片空白之地。或许那里土地荒芜，不宜居住？无论如何，有关它的秘密都已经被历史埋葬。
E: What can be found in the northeast are nothing but barren lands. Perhaps those lands are just too desolate to be habitable? Whatever the case might be, its secrets remain deeply buried in history.

C: 看向西方。
E: Look to the west.

C: 啊哦，出汁了。好吧，它不是造景。对不起。
E: Oh, wow — some sap. Alright, it's not fake. I'm sorry.

C: 那就让我看看柜子里有什么…
E: Then let's take a look at what's in the closet...

C: 我的机器人从你这里学会如何说话之后，似乎毫无进步……
E: My robot didn't seem to make any improvements after learning how to speak from you...

C: 它的言谈举止非常分裂，有时候盛气凌人，有时又特别温柔，偶尔还会对我不理不睬……
E: It's very disjointed when it speaks, sometimes domineering but sometimes gentle — and sometimes even ignoring me...

C: 错误。你听到一阵沉闷的滋滋声，四个凹槽内的数字恢复到了初始的、无序的状态。糟糕——看来你得从头再试一次了。
E: Wrong. You hear a low buzzing sound as the numbers in all four slots return to their original random state. Shoot, looks like you gotta start all over again.
Three.

C: 刀的长度显然超过了你的身高，你思忖了片刻，还是放过自己吧。
E: The weapon is longer than your height. You mull it over for a second before deciding to spare yourself.

C: （把手伸进罐子里。）
E: (Put your hand into the vase.)

C: （算了，以后再说。）
E: (Maybe later.)

C: 高见…
E: Esteemed opinion...

C: 「与仙舟一刀两断，远走高飞！虱多不痒，债多不愁，随它去便是！」
E: "Cut off all ties to Xianzhou, and run for the hills! Trouble never comes alone, so don't worry about those debts — just let them all go!"

C: 你自己怎么不去修？
E: Why can't you fix it yourself?

C: 说来惭愧…自从做了管理岗以后，我已经很久没有自己实操了，手艺生疏…你懂吧？
E: This is embarrassing... Ever since I took on a management position, I haven't had much hands-on experience. Use it or lose it. You know what I'm saying?

C: 没问题。
E: No problem.

C: 以后一定。
E: Next time for sure.

C: …您真是太好了！我们这就出发。
E: ...You're so nice! Let's hit the road right away.

C: 这样吧客人，刚才<color=#dbc291ff>「赎珠阁」门前走过了一位青年</color>，一直念叨着「复仇、复仇…」，你去问问他，也许会有些收获。
E: How about this, then? Just now, a <color=#dbc291ff>young man just passed by the door of the Jeweler's Pagoda</color> muttering on about "Revenge... Revenge..." Go ask him. It might just be worth your while.

C: 陌生人，在此相逢也算是有缘…收下这些吧，这些财物是我们与原生家庭最后的联结，将它们交给你，我们二人便与那两家人再无关系了！
E: Well, stranger, I guess it's fate that we met each other here, too... Take these. They're the last remaining ties we have to our families. If you take these, then the two of us will have nothing more to do with either of our families!

C: 再说了，我们希望你也要继续玩啊。
E: Besides, we hope that you can keep playing as well.

C: 你说的都对…
E: Everything you said was true...

C: 是啊，直到现在我也记忆犹新。
E: He sure was. I can still remember him vividly to this day.

C: 听上去，您似乎和奎茵相处得不错。那么，您可以听听我的不情之请吗？
E: Sounds like you and Quinn are getting along like a house on fire. So, if I may be so bold as to ask you a favor?

C: 不如爱情故事。
E: I prefer love stories.

C: 没什么。
E: Nothing.

C: 浪漫一点的…那就说说小行星带的事吧。
E: More romantic... Then let's talk about the incident in the asteroid belt.

C: 今天非得给她揪出来不可！
E: We'll flush her out today, for sure!

C: （小声）大哥，这家伙说的有道理，上头现在肯定不愿意跟家族撕破脸皮。
E: (Whispering) Dude, {M#he}{F#she} makes sense. The repercussions might be too risky for the higher-ups.

C: 那天，我们的飞船在一颗沙漠星球补给物资。仓管从当地人手里买来了一头名叫大毛的生物——那是它的名字。至于它究竟是什么物种，我不知道。
E: That day, our spaceship was restocking provisions on a desert planet. The warehouse manager bought a creature named Hairy from the locals — that was its name. I don't know what species it was.

C: 但大毛不是牲畜。它和我们一样，是拥有高级智慧的生物…我知道这说法有些奇怪，可当一头牛开始谈论灵魂与哲学时，真有人能把它单纯当作肉来看待吗？
E: But Hairy was no livestock. Just like us, Hairy was a creature of high intelligence... I know it's strange to say, but when a cow starts talking about spirits and philosophy with you, how can anyone treat it as just a piece of meat?

C: 现在我每次出发前都会带上超多粮食——只有让人饱腹到看见肉就反胃，他们才不会见什么都想尝尝味道。
E: Now I make sure that I pack plenty of extra rations whenever I head out. If people are full when they see meat, they won't be compelled to try and eat everything they see.

C: 那位老人则抱着一盆重新抽枝的花，喜极而泣——那是他亡妻留下的「三日卷柏」，只要一点阳光就能起死回生。
E: The elderly man cried tears of joy as he clutched a pot of flowers, its branch sprouting anew — it was the three-day spikemoss left by his late wife, which could be brought back to life with only a little sunlight.

C: 可我不是筑梦师。
E: But I'm not a Dreamweaver.

C: 来吧，赶紧动起来，脚下还有一方热土等着我们去建设！
E: Come on. Let's get our hands dirty! The land beneath us is waiting to be developed!

C: 大概一百多年以前吧，我还在上学的时候，一群小屁孩聚在一块聊过未来的梦想——
E: About a century ago when I was still a student, my friends and I gathered and talked about our dreams.

C: 我招募了一个*志同道合的伙伴*，辛辛苦苦几番奔走经营，可眼见都要成功了……
E: I enlisted the help of a "like-minded partner" and worked like a dog for the project. And just as we were about to succeed...

C: 嗤，我还说自己是奥斯瓦尔多·施耐德呢，你信吗？<color=#f29e38ff>至于他那份企划究竟有多么「精彩绝伦」，我倒是建议你自己弄来看看。</color>
E: Tsk. Would you believe me if I told you I were Oswaldo Schneider? <color=#f29e38ff>As for how "marvelously outstanding" his designs are, well, I suggest you take a look yourself once you get your hands on them.</color>

C: 那群傻*同事也是，跟着这种嫉贤妒能、邀功诿过的上司，能有什么出路……
E: I don't understand why my co-workers are still sticking with a boss who's jealous of talented people and takes credit for everything. What kind of future do they expect when they work for Feinman...

C: 第二！你当然站着说话不腰疼——它卖的又不是你的竞品，影响的又不是你的业绩！
E: And besides, that billboard was not promoting your competitor's product or messing with your business! Of course you can just play the good guy!

C: 我的要求明明不高…我只希望能得到尊重！当然，最好还能多赚点钱……
E: I'm not asking for much... All I want is a bit of respect! And, of course, some money, if possible...

C: 我…怎么了？
E: What's wrong... with me?

C: 你回来了？拿到我想要的东西了吗？
E: You're back? Did you get what I wanted?

C: 男人安静地表达着他那二十分之一的浓厚爱意——复制品科蒂在他身旁不为所动。
E: The man pours out his heart, expressing his one-twentieth of intense love, while the replica Coty remains unfazed.

C: 我曾经爱上过一个人——她是纯粹的享乐派，银河间最擅长玩乐的游戏女王。
E: I once fell for someone who was all about pure hedonism, the queen of video games.

C: 只是回想起来，我就禁不住想要微笑…她那么完美，和我是那么合拍…我们就像一条绿枝上结出的两朵鲜花，互相映衬，互相欣赏……
E: Just thinking about it brings a smile to my face. She was so perfect, so in sync with me... We were like two flowers blooming on the same branch, reflecting and appreciating each other...

C: 是宾客啊。多谢关心，只是工作上遇到了些烦心事——
E: Oh, thank you for your concern, dear guest. I've been having some trouble with my work...

C: 太憋屈了，辞职吧。
E: This is just too suffocating. You should quit.

C: 我的…我的绒球？哈哈，宾客，你可真是幽默啊。
E: My... my fluff ball? Haha, you've got a great sense of humor, dear guest.

C: 好了，快点讲吧，芙尔拉女士到底想让你说什么？
E: Well, speak up now. What did Madam Forla want you to say?

C: 对！都怪这些消费者不能接受更多元的艺术——要想帮助艺者实现梦想，就必须要先改变他们的审美！
E: Exactly! It's the fault of those consumers who aren't receptive to more diverse art! If I want to help my talents realize their dreams, I must convince the consumers to change their preferences first!

C: 拉洛丝，怎么了？你难道不想唱你最喜欢的歌曲吗？
E: What's the matter, Larose? Don't you want to perform the songs you like?

C: 下次再聊。
E: Let's catch up another time.

C: 我本来没想射她的，但那辆车颠簸得太厉害，开枪的机会又转瞬即逝……
E: It wasn't my intention to shoot her. The car was so bumpy, and the opportunity to fire would be gone in a fleeting second...

C: 悲剧不会落幕，只会换个形式开场……
E: A tragedy does not conclude but rather takes on a different form to begin anew...

C: 呵，水哪有苏乐达好喝？来匹诺康尼就得喝苏乐达。
E: Water can't compare to the taste of SoulGlad! You've got to have SoulGlad when you're in Penacony.

C: 不过，说到苏乐达嘛……
E: Speaking of SoulGlad...

C: 不对，你是…诺卡？真的是你吗？
E: Hold on. Are you... Noka? Is it really you?

C: 也许吧，但…总感觉莫名不真切。
E: Maybe, but... it still feels surreal to me.

C: 只要你帮我完成「取材」，我甚至可以让你成为下一部电影的主角！
E: As long as you help me gather inspiration, I can make you the protagonist of my next movie!

C: 所以，我希望你能帮我拍几张照片，让我把这些影像铭刻进存储系统里。
E: I hope you can take a few photos for me so that I can keep them in my storage system.

C: 你死了？
E: You died?

C: 那东西可以是奇迹、是伟人、是远在天外的星神，甚至是…强大到足以终结战争的武器。
E: It can be a miracle, a respectable figure, a faraway Aeon, or even a weapon powerful enough to end the war.

E: The Academy's studies have reached its plateau. All trashcans will face the challenge of finding meaning.

C: 什么？哈努兄弟才不会做毁坏公物的事情。你、你竟敢玷污他！
E: What? Hanu would never damage public property! H—How dare you tarnish his name!

C: 爽快！拿上这个之后，你肯定能比「泯灭帮」走得更远！
E: Speedy! You'll definitely be stronger than the Annihilation Gang with this!

C: 遥想当年，整个街区的人可都要排队向我们进献垃圾，再看现在，他们甚至不会垃圾分类！——可怜了兄弟们，还要来这种地方陪我受苦。
E: Back in the day, all the people on this street were queuing up to offer us tributes of trash. Look at them now. They don't even know how to sort their trash! I pity my brothers the most, who have to endure this suffering with me in this place.

C: 我见你的样子，到此地应该有段时间了。老夫初来乍到，桶生地不熟，有些事情还望你不吝赐教。
E: You must have been here for some time. I haven't been here long, and am unfamiliar with this place. I actually hope that you can advise me on some matters.

C: 你说的每一句话我都视为真理，这正是我开悟的秘诀！我知道了，你信不过我，来，咱们练几招你就知道了！
E: My secret to enlightenment is simple: I just viewed every word that came out of your mouth as the truth! I know that you don't believe me, so... let's spar for a few rounds. You'll definitely notice the difference!

C: 「桶制作学院」！为了在垃圾桶王国找份好工作总要优先考虑的学院！来吧，现在是入学考试！
E: Cancrafting Academy! To land a decent job in the Trashcan Kingdom, our academy should be your first consideration! Come, now is the time for the entrance exam!

C: 说了这么多，最后告诉你一个秘密——其实，我也没上过学！
E: After saying so much, let me tell you a secret — honestly, I've never attended school!

C: 替白厄写回信吧。
E: Write a reply for Phainon.

C: 于是，我打点好家里，修理好信箱和屋子，又做好口粮。这次我再也没有借口了，就算没有他陪我一起，我也要跨出那一步迷！
E: So I cleaned up the house, repaired the mailbox and the roof, and packed my food. This time, I have no more excuses. Even if he's not by my side, I'm taking that first step, memi!

C: 呜嗡——
E: Urrhh...

C: 多好的孩子啊~你一定是迷路了，对不对？乖，别害怕。
E: What a sweet kid~ You must be lost, huh? Don't be scared, it's okay.

C: 点点红色从叶片间绽放而出，那是新生的花朵。
E: Tiny specks of red peek out from between the leaves — newborn flowers awakening.

C: 感觉你对这里还挺熟悉。
E: You seem familiar with this place.

C: 于是，你也沉浸在视线那端的风光。远山…帆船…海平线……
E: So, you also admire the scenery before you. Mountains faraway... Sailboats... The horizon over the sea...

C: 不要急嘛~稍微离开一阵子，也许只是睁眼、闭眼的重逢，就有全新的邂逅等待着我们呐~
E: Don't rush! Maybe if we take a little break, a reunion that feels like just the blink of an eye could lead to a whole new encounter waiting for us.

C: 但我肩负部族最后的期望，若是临阵脱逃，日后有何面目祭奠他们，又该以什么名义继续苟活于世？
E: But I bear the final hope of my people. If I were to desert, what right would I have to uphold their memory? What name would I live under in this world?

C: 他是…灰色头发和胡子的男性…挺老的，不像是会打仗的人……
E: He... He is a man with gray hair and a gray beard... He's quite old, and he doesn't look like someone fit for battle...

C: 你只是想找个安静地方躺着而已，至于回应信徒的祈祷这种麻烦事，还是留给那些愿为神圣事业献身的高尚人士吧。你翻了个身，打算换个更舒服的姿势。
E: You just wanted to find a quiet place to lie down. When it comes to answering the prayers of disciples, leave that to the noble folks who are willing to devote themselves to their holy calling. You turn over, trying to find a more comfortable position.

C: 莫名地，你突然开始怀念起在木马肚子里度过的时光，安静、幽暗，带着些微的温暖，你意识到那就是你始终在追寻的家的感觉。
E: Suddenly, you are overcome with a mysterious nostalgia for the time that you spent inside the wooden horse's stomach. The quiet, the darkness, the subtle warmth... Subconsciously, you realize that it was the feeling of home that you had been searching for.

C: 奥赫玛。
E: Okhema.

C: …去往「布利萨」的那个。
E: ...The one bound for Bulsa.

C: 飘渺的声音仍在空中回荡，但那匹木马早已不在身旁——它确实将你送达了新的场所。
E: The mysterious voice still lingers in the air, but the wooden horse has disappeared — having successfully transported you elsewhere.

C: 我、我想去找爸爸妈妈……
E: I... I wanna find mom and dad...

C: 于是他们在建筑的窗洞上放上香料，不消言语，大地兽便会循着香茅将他们送到酒馆，循着木檀将他们送去城墙。丁香是店铺，栀子是学堂，玫瑰则是情人的住所。
E: So they placed spices in the windows of their buildings, without a word, the dromases would follow the scent of lemongrass to take them to the tavern, or sandalwood to guide them to the city walls. Clove led to shops, gardenia to schools, and rose to the homes of their lovers.

C: …果然，以上定义指向{RUBY_B#第<unbreak>287</unbreak>次「再创世」}实验#287{RUBY_E#}、{RUBY_B#第<unbreak>8745</unbreak>次「再创世」}实验#8745{RUBY_E#}及{RUBY_B#第<unbreak>134962</unbreak>次「再创世」}实验#134962{RUBY_E#}中标签为「海洋」的黄金裔模型。推测成立。
E: ...Confirmed. These definitions correlate with the "Ocean" variant Chrysos Heir models in the {RUBY_B#287th Era Nova}Experiment #287{RUBY_E#}, {RUBY_B#8745th Era Nova}Experiment #8745{RUBY_E#}, and {RUBY_B#134,<unbreak>962</unbreak>nd Era Nova}Experiment #134,<unbreak>962</unbreak>{RUBY_E#}. Speculation substantiated.

C: 「对称占用式逻辑编码」，简称「死锁」。
E: "Symmetrical Occupancy Logic Encoding," which is commonly termed, the "Deadlock."

C: （教它握手和坐下）
E: (Teach it to shake and sit)

C: 你爱她？「她」是谁？
E: Are you in love? Who is "she"?

C: 这次军团中的敌人，是反物质融铸而成的银河兵器——你知道这意味着什么吗？
E: The enemies in the Legion are cosmic weapons made of antimatter. Do you know what that means?

C: 只要我还在空间站一日，就不会允许那些孽障在黑塔大人的地盘肆意撒野！
E: So long as I'm in the space station, I will never let them ravage the territory of Madam Herta!

C: 第九杯…加油，埃美丽。这不算什么，只要捏着鼻子就能喝下去。
E: The ninth cup... Come on, Emily. This is nothing. Just pinch your nose and drink it.

C: 我还有些问题想问你…
E: I've got something to ask you.

C: 除了波少，谁还可以不走流程就拿到奇物？谁还可以自行挑选奇物？你以为你这样初来乍到的新人，就能拥有波少的学识和水平吗？
E: Except for Master Capote, no one gets Curios without going through the normal process. No one gets to choose Curios. You think a newbie like you can match Master Capote's knowledge and standard?

C: 喂，你这幅不信任又担心的表情是怎么回事？
E: Hey, what's that distrustful and concerned look on your face?

C: 总之这次的事件十分感谢你愿意帮助小姐。如果之后还有特殊情况，也希望你可以为她伸出援手。当然…这次的这种事件还是别再发生了。
E: Anyways, thanks for helping Lady Asta out. I hope you'll lend a hand again when she finds herself in difficult situations in the future. That said, the fewer the situations, the better...

C: 就是这只爪子太不争气了，烦死了，就不能长灵活点嘛！
E: But this paw of mine is getting under my skin! Why can't they be more flexible!?

C: 你的名字好特别…
E: You have a special name...

C: 从名字上你大概就知道我在这里的地位，所以，要找我闲聊可以，但你得掂量掂量自己的分量，懂了吗？
E: You can probably tell I'm the bigwig here from my name. I don't chat with every Tom, Dick, and Harry I run into. You know what I'm saying?

C: 这就对了，当时我就是在这里七进七出，将那些虫子打得落花流水，才换来这里的安宁！
E: That's how you're supposed to feel. I once came in and out of here seven times to defeat the bugs in return for the peace here!

C: 呜呜在这，很熟呜！
E: I'm here! Very familiar, woo!

C: 你是从哪儿溜进来的？
E: Where did you sneak in from?

C: 小机器人为你播放了一段影像，你看到几只活蹦乱跳的「阮•梅」造物正在试图与它沟通，但它机械地重复着清扫工作，不予回应。
E: The little robot plays a video for you, showing several energetic creations of Ruan Mei trying to communicate with it. The robot, however, only mechanically repeats its cleaning task and did not respond.

C: 我似乎忘记了我们之间的一些事。
E: I seem to have forgotten something about us.

C: 我的球棒有话要说。
E: My bat has something to say about that.

C: 我都看到裂缝了…
E: I can already see cracks...

C: 无稽之谈，这可是渎神！…都是同行，您何必在这拆我的台呢。
E: Groundless accusations bordering on blasphemy! ...Why would you malign a compatriot in the same field of work?

C: 没错！没有仲裁人见证，即便天平发生了倾斜，塔兰顿也绝不会认可！
E: That's right! Without an arbitrator as witness, even if the scales weren't even, Talanton would also never acknowledge this!

C: 击打寻常盾牌，以「最强之矛」；击打「最强之盾」，以寻常利器。
E: Strike an ordinary shield, with the strongest spear one will. Strike the strongest shield, with an ordinary weapon one shall.

C: 只要会编，到处都是商机！
E: As long as we can spin, the world is paved with opportunity!

C: 在下定决心亲自举行仪式后，你认真研读了卷轴上的内容。
E: Having made up your mind to carry out the ceremony personally, you begin to carefully study the contents of the scroll.

C: 从上面记录的内容看，这似乎是某种神秘仪式的步骤，附带了信徒们的批注。
E: From the content recorded inside, it seems that these are instructions for some kind of mysterious ceremony, attached with notes made by disciples.

C: 要不你们先做个自我介绍？
E: How about you introduce yourselves first?

C: 哈，哲学辩论的结果，岂是由人证的多少来决定的？若真是如此，公民大会就将是这世上最富有智慧的机构——他们永远都是对的。
E: Hah. So a philosophical conclusion is proven simply by the number of people that believe it? If that were the case, then the citizens' assembly would be the most intelligent organization on earth... They would always be correct.

C: 遇到麻烦了吗？
E: Something wrong?

C: 你拎起软垫，掂了掂，发现这玩意比看起来要更重一些。你努力在不破坏软垫的前提下将它拆开，发现里面是一块<color=#dbc291ff>石板</color>。
E: You pick up the small cushion and weigh it in your hand to discover that it's heavier than it looks. As you try to open it up without damaging anything, you uncover a <color=#dbc291ff>slate</color> tucked inside.

C: …好…好孩子…波瑟芬妮…谢谢…我的波瑟芬妮……
E: ...Thank you... Good child... Possephone... My Possephone...

C: 我原本还打算请她吃点好的来着，结果她却说躺着吃东西容易积食……
E: I was going to treat her to something delicious, but she told me she'd get indigestion if she ate while lying down...

C: 现在…我又能做些什么呢？我又该做点什么呢？
E: So now... what can I do? What should I do?

C: 去找卫兵自首吧，为你过去的行为付出代价，相信他们不会为难你的。
E: Go and turn yourself in to the guards, and pay the price for your previous actions. I don't think they'll take it too hard on you.

C: 是你啊，{F#女士}{M#先生}！我现在正在跟弥斯姐姐学习演奏水漫琴。
E: Oh it's you, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}! I'm learning how to play the water lyre with Miss Misia.

C: 我的指法不怎么样，但唱得挺好听。弥斯姐姐建议我只学简单的旋律伴奏，把精力放在演唱上。
E: My technique isn't great, but I can sing pretty well. Miss Misia wants me to focus on just learning simple accompanying melodies, and focus more on singing and performing.

C: 是我。
E: It's me.

C: 这不是巧了！
E: What a coincidence!

C: 你的逻辑可真是一团浆糊。我知道你儿子在奥赫玛混得不错，何来无处可去一说？
E: Your logic makes no sense! I know your son is getting by pretty well in Okhema. What do you mean, "you have nowhere to go"?

C: 老犟种、老倔牛！
E: That bullheaded old man! Stubborn as a mule!

C: 哎，这实验没办法推进下去啊…这都大半个月了，机组还没修好吗？
E: Ack, my experiment can't go on without it... It's been two weeks already. Can't they fix it?

C: 我上次教你的话术呢？
E: What about those public speaking tips I gave you last time?

C: …给它演奏音乐试试。
E: ...Try playing music for it.

C: 嗡嗡…呜嗡嗡！呜嗡嗡嗡嗡！
E: Grumphhh... Grumphhh! Grumphhhh!

C: 失恋的大地兽再次从你们面前呼啸而过，只不过现在多了好几个跟在后面的人。
E: The heartbroken dromas charges past once more, but now it's followed by several people trailing behind.

C: （总之，假装对上嘴型吧…）
E: (Just mouth along...)

C: 这张壁画描绘的是…刻法勒接过黎明机器的场景？我好像在克帖西芬姐姐的书画集里看到过……
E: This mural depicts... Kephale receiving the "Dawn Device"? I think I saw that in Miss Ctesiphona's art collection...

C: 贵宾，你说…刻法勒真的能听到我们说话吗？
E: Dear guest... Do you think Kephale can really hear our prayers?

C: 别说这种傻话，兄弟！我们不是约好了要一起站上奥赫玛小贩业的顶点吗？
E: Don't say such foolish things, brother! Didn't we promise to reach the pinnacle of the Okhema marketplace together?

C: 我也要报仇…可恶的怪物，我想好说服祭司的说辞全没用了！
E: I want to get my revenge too... those blasted monsters ruined all the reasoning I had prepared to convince the priest!

C: 我就知道。
E: I knew it.

C: 你如砍瓜切菜般将怪物收拾干净。
E: You cut through the monsters like slicing through melons and vegetables, effortlessly clearing them out.

C: 你和柯库翁将盾牌摆放好。
E: You and Glykon go set up the shields.

C: 嗯…效果还可以。等他们带着盾牌和矛回来，就不必担心黑潮的袭击了。
E: Hmm... looks good enough. When they come back with shields and lances, we'll be safe from the black tide's intrusion.

C: 希望他们平安归来。
E: Hope they make it back in one piece.

C: 别对他们的货抱太大希望。
E: Don't expect too much from their merchandise.

C: 呵呵……
E: Hehe...

C: 两位小贩背起行囊，左手持矛，右手持盾，朝中庭冲来——
E: The two merchants lift their packs, gripping lances in their left hands and shields in their right, charging into the courtyard—

C: 他们真的很有信念感。
E: They had such convictions.

C: 别忘了我的致谢词。
E: Don't forget to mention me in your speech.

C: 要适应英雄的身份。
E: You have to adapt to your role as a hero.

C: 这个老顽固……
E: This stubborn old guy...

C: 但孩童诚挚的目光让人无法拒绝，你计划先找到她口中那位「好心的守卫叔叔」，打听一下消息。
E: But the sincere gaze of a child is a hard thing to resist. You plan to find that "Mr. Nice Guard" she talked about first and get some details.

C: 哈哈哈，别逗了，你是不是看现在战事紧张，故意说笑话让我们心情轻松轻松。
E: Ha! Stop joking around. Say, you're not deliberately jesting to lift our moods with how desperately the war's going, right?

C: 时间刚刚好，希瑞雅他们还没到，我先去后面藏起来。
E: Perfect timing. I'll hide before Serena and them arrive.

C: 嗯，我带你回安全的地方吧！
E: Well, I'll escort you back.

C: 嗯…个中缘由，{NICKNAME}说、说不定已经猜出来了。
E: Hmm... you m—might have already figured out the reason why.

C: 你…没事吧？
E: You... are you okay?

C: 或许可以抽空帮帮忙？你打算——
E: Maybe you could help out when you get a chance? You're planning to—

C: 看看别的。
E: Look at another one.

C: 之后要忙起来了，请原谅我先行告退，二位还请慢慢参观。
E: I've got to run now, business calls. Please, make yourselves at home and feel free to look around.

C: 为什么{NICKNAME}你可以选择坐下，但我做不到。
E: {NICKNAME}, how come it lets you sit on it, but not me?

C: {NICKNAME}，你要是有空，可以坐在车窗这儿，关上灯，仔细看看窗外的景色。
E: {NICKNAME}, if you get any time, you can sit here in the window seat, turn off the lights, and have a nice long gaze out the window.

C: 只是…如果真有一天，下层区的人们不再需要我这个医生了，大家都能够无忧无虑地健康生活下去……
E: Of course, if there is a day when the Underworld doesn't need a doctor like me anymore and everyone lives happily ever after...

C: 星星好像糖果哦！
E: The stars are like candies!

C: …啊！克拉拉完全没想到…谢谢{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！
E: Ah! How come I never thought of that? Thanks {NICKNAME}!

C: 刚刚，瓦尔特先生给克拉拉讲了好多好多、其他星球的机器人电影故事！
E: I just finished talking to Mr. Yang. He was telling me about movies on other planets that have robots in them!

C: 还是要谢谢{NICKNAME}邀请我过来——虽然我现在暂时脱不开身…但我也很期待列车之旅哦！
E: Thanks for inviting me, {NICKNAME}. I've got my hands full right now, but I'm looking forward to the Express tour!

C: 所以——我就来找你们玩啦！怎么样，这么久没见，有没有想姐姐啊～
E: But at least I have time to come visit you guys! Did you miss me?

C: 抱歉，刚才是我在自言自语，让你产生了误会。目前列车上十分安全，没有…不对，只有我一个外来人员。
E: Oh, sorry, I was thinking out loud. Don't worry, the Express is perfectly safe — there are no... Come to think of it, I'm an unregistered visitor.

C: 你不是立体投影吗？
E: Aren't you using a hologram, though?

C: 发生了什么？
E: What happened?

C: 嘿嘿，不然的话，下次你就见不到这么可爱的神医大人啦。
E: Hehe, they better be... or I won't be coming back!

C: 不知道为什么，从列车上回去之后，我开始做梦了。
E: I don't know why, but since I left the Express, I started having dreams.

C: 书？我看的书…还算多，不过都是和星神有关的文献。
E: Books? I do read... a lot, but those books are all related to Aeons.

C: 《星神的失落——银河民俗考》丛书挺不错的，通俗易懂…就是篇幅长了点……
E: The series The Fall of The Aeons – A Discussion on Cosmic Traditions is pretty good and easy to understand. It's just a bit long...

C: 你有点神经紧张。
E: You seem rather nervous.

C: 那些人是谁？
E: Who are those people you're talking about?

C: 关于卡波特…
E: About Capote...

C: 说到这个…老师，彦卿最近还注意到了一件事！
E: Speaking of which, I noticed something recently!

C: 他性格内敛。
E: He's just a bit reserved.

C: 怎么样？是不是已经不会感觉到咄咄逼人的气势，也不会觉得话题被聊死无法进行了？
E: How is it? You don't feel I'm so aggressive and difficult to talk to anymore, right?

C: 你在写什么报告书？
E: What report are you writing?

C: 波少粉丝后援会解散了吗？
E: Did the Master Capote Fan Club disband?

C: 继续探索下去吧。
E: Never stop exploring.
Hmm...

C: {NICKNAME}，有事的话就尽管去忙吧？不用在意我，我只想再多看一会儿这外面的景色。
E: {NICKNAME}, please, don't mind me. Feel free to go about your business. I think I'll stay here and enjoy the view a while longer.

C: 希儿喜欢这个风格？
E: You... like the style here?

C: 不过等这阵子事情忙完，我也许会来观景车厢里实践实践这样消磨光阴的法子。
E: However, when work does slow down, I may return to the parlor car to put such leisure into practice.

C: …好、好主意。
E: ...G—Good idea.

C: 确实有人，就在那边！
E: There's actually someone right over there!

C: 虽然雪衣大人当着我的面说过，可我以为她只是在安慰我。
E: She did tell me that face to face... but I thought she was just trying to comfort me...

C: 不过，要是工作太艰难了，我、我还是会考虑申请离职的……
E: But, um, if work gets too tough, I might still consider quitting...

C: 没事没事，你就收下吧。
E: It's fine, really, take them.

C: 其实，来到这里之前，我经常会梦见一辆在银河间穿行的列车。璀璨的群星在它身边掠过，在梦里，我不愿意离开，会一直一直趴在车窗上看着外面的景色。但醒来之后，它就消失了。
E: Actually, I often dream of a train traveling through the cosmos, with brilliant stars whizzing by. I'd always be glued to the window, gazing outside. I dreamed I'd never leave, but once I woke up, it would all be gone.

C: 很棒！它们虽然风格各异，但都让人沉迷。
E: It's awesome! Each song has its own style, but they all draw you in.

C: 我也很期待匹诺康尼的音乐。
E: I'm also looking forward to hearing the music of Penacony.

C: 请别这么说…能够引起人的共鸣和遐想，这才是音乐的魅力所在。
E: There's no need to say that... The beauty of music is its ability to resonate with people and stir up their imaginations.

C: 我也时刻提醒自己：想要拥有完美的舞台，务必要在保持演唱技巧的同时，让听众们感受到歌曲中蕴含的情感和故事。
E: I constantly remind myself: If I want to have a perfect performance, I have to let the audience feel the emotions and story of the song as well.

C: 这就点起来~你看！是不是很好看！
E: Let's light them up! Look! Aren't they nice!

C: 我不做梦。
E: I don't dream.

C: 第一组，金色。
E: The first set, the gold deck.

C: 我看到了充满荆棘的花丛，舒适的躺椅。鸟儿在笼中歌唱，厨师奉上可口的茶点，乐师演奏着动人的篇章……
E: I see a shrub full of thorns and a comfortable reclining chair. Birds sing in cages, a chef offers delicious treats, a musician plays a moving piece...

C: 它们如此绚丽多彩，实在难以评判。不知道列车上是否有方法可以鉴别种子和晶石的真伪？
E: They glimmer with such brilliance. It's really hard to determine. I wonder if there is some way to appraise the authenticity of the seeds and Rainbowite? Perhaps right here on the Express?

C: 要不休息一会儿吧？
E: Why don't you take a break?

C: 嗯，嗯嗯！我现在就去帮你问问看。
E: Okay! I'll go ask for you right now.

C: 「老铁」喜欢听新闻？
E: Old Mettle likes to listen to the news?

C: 让恩公见笑了。只是小女子也算是从他人言语中重构了自我的认知，所以才有如此联想。
E: I've made a fool of myself, haven't I? However, I essentially reconstructed my own cognition from the words of others, which is why I entertained that thought.

C: 界种科为培育出的植物提供了低温与干燥的密闭器具，以确保植物的长时间保存与活性。
E: The Department of Ecology keeps the grown plants in low-temperature, dry, and airtight containers to ensure long-term preservation and vitality.

C: 呵呵，你真幽默。行政区本地每家人我都熟悉，可从没见过你。哎～人人都爱往行政区跑，可我们这些本地人啊，都住腻咯。
E: Hehe, you're a funny one. I know every local family in the Administrative District, yet I've never seen you before. *sigh* Everyone wants to come to this district, but for us locals, we've grown tired of living here...

C: 什么意思？
E: What's that supposed to mean?

C: 啊，抱歉，我并不是有意冒犯你。我叫莉拉，是《远行者》月刊的撰稿人，也是一名动物学家。
E: Ah, sorry, I didn't mean to offend you. My name is Lila. I'm a writer for the Voyager, and also a zoologist.

C: 不客气，为每位市民服务是我的职责所在。
E: It's okay. It is my duty to serve every citizen.

C: 好了，现在请给我一点私人时间……
E: Now, if you could just give me some privacy...

C: 怎么又来了？都说了现在是休息时间…哎，这位市民，你到底想问什么事？
E: You again? I just said I'm on break... Fine, so what exactly do you want to know?

C: 一万字的采访稿，被我生生地扩成了四万字，这已经是极限了！
E: I've already bloated a ten thousand-word interview script to forty thousand! That's the best I can do.

C: 连我都已经购置了两套！我还能自己骗自己吗？
E: I myself have bought two properties! I can't be scamming myself, can I?

C: 你回来啦？之前跟你说的城郊楼盘你考虑得怎么样了，来一套吧。
E: Good to see you back. Did you have time to consider buying the property we talked about?

C: 还有什么想问的吗？
E: Anything else you'd like to ask?

C: 但我可以保证！这部书绝没有任何不良内容，它只是…太有想象力了！它描绘了雪幕之外的种种宏伟奇景…简直精彩绝伦！
E: But I can assure you, there is no problem with the content! It's just... too creative. By that I mean it depicts the wonders beyond the snow curtain. Truly magnificent work.

C: 你真的是科学家吗？
E: Are you really a scientist?

C: 可别不信！你想啊——下层区断联这么多年现在又突然开放，其背后的原因我看一定就是大宇宙时间隧道——以此类推可知……
E: Don't doubt me! Think about it. Contact with the Underworld has been cut off for so many years, and now the Underworld is suddenly open. I believe that the reason behind all this must be the Big Universe time tunnel. From this deduction, we know that...

C: 你是打算退隐吗？
E: Do you plan on retiring?

C: 给我介绍下这些花吧。
E: What about these flowers?

C: …不过，这真的是我想要的生活吗？我当初拼死拼活挤进铁卫技术部，可不是为了躺着混吃等死的。
E: ...Is this really the life that I want? Back then, I did everything I could to get into the Technology Division, but I didn't do all that just so I could lie back and wait for my life to be over.

C: 收到礼物确实会很开心，不过要是数量太多的话，我也会觉得有点不好意思呢…但我也不方便拒绝大家的好意，就只好都收下啦。
E: I'm happy to get gifts but sometimes there are too many, and I feel pressured... It's not nice to say no to gifts, so I accept them all.

C: 谢谢你，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}。
E: Thank you, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}.

C: 而我挣脱束缚的方式就是——睡觉！通过这种低消耗的行为来审视自己的人生，远离外界的纷扰……
E: My way of breaking the cycle is to... sleep! I stay clear of the world's disturbances through this low-cost act and I regularly reflect on my life...

C: 数量有限，先到先得，老顾客重复购买有九折优惠！
E: Limited quantity, first-come, first-served. <unbreak>10%</unbreak> discount for regular customers!

C: 嘿，看你那副沮丧的表情…被我识破了吧！
E: Haha, your pathetic face tells me that... I saw through your trick.

C: 总之，费斯曼老爹的事就是我们鼹鼠党的事。我身为鼹鼠党二把手，必须好好夸奖你一番，荣誉队员！
E: After all, Fersman's problem is the Moles' problem, too. And as the Moles' deputy, I really must praise you, o trusty honorary member!

C: …这你都不知道吗？发生了…大事！
E: ...You don't know? Something BIG has happened!

C: 没什么事就算了…
E: Okay.

C: 哦，你来啦！你瞧，我的机器人已经造好啦。
E: Oh, here you are! Look! I've finished building my robot.

C: 甭客气，来了下层区就都是我的客人嘛。有什么需要尽管说！
E: No worries. You are now my guest since you are in the Underworld. If you need anything, just let me know!

C: 麻烦的是那些大件。一般来说，我得把他们彻底拆解，仔细检查一遍才行。
E: The bigger items are the real trouble. Generally speaking, I have to disassemble them and check everything carefully.

C: 提前放工了？
E: Early day off?

C: 况且，只要我发出需要帮助的讯号…我知道她一定会回到我身边。这样就够了。
E: Besides, if I send a signal asking for her help... I know she'll return to me. And that's all I need.

C: 虽然现在的贝洛伯格千疮百孔…但只要观察得足够细致，就总能发现它迷人的一面。
E: Despite Belobog being in a terrible state these days... You can always find its charming side, if only you look carefully enough.

C: 真不知道布洛妮娅是怎么做到的——每天翻看那么多文件，听那么多人在耳边叨叨…换成我的话，迟早会疯掉。
E: I have no idea how Bronya does it — poring over all those documents every day, having to listen to all that idle chit-chat... I would've gone mad long ago.

C: 我知道，「地火」的组建原本就是为了弥补银鬃铁卫的缺席，如果上下层区恢复了往常的样子，那「地火」也就没必要存在了。
E: I know that Wildfire was originally formed due to the absence of any Silvermane Guards. If things are back to normal between the Overworld and the Underworld, then there's no need for Wildfire to keep going on.

C: 过去，任何问题似乎都可以靠拳头解决——不管发生了什么，我手里这柄镰刀就是硬道理。
E: In the past, it seemed that you could solve any problem with your fists... No matter what happened, the scythe in my hand made the rules.

C: 托你们的福，这里的人们重新找回了希望。和上层的贸易渠道重新打通以后，很多此前浑浑度日的人也重拾了生活的动力。
E: With your help, the people here have regained hope. Reopening trade routes with the Overworld has given life new meaning for people who were once struggling just to get by.

C: 不过我也不打算卯着劲挣钱——人生苦短，这一辈子光挣钱有什么意思？有时间还不如多搞搞自己的兴趣爱好呢。
E: But I don't plan on just working for a crust — life is short, so what's the point of just living to make money? If I've got the time, I'd rather be tinkering on my hobbies.

C: 回家看过了吗？
E: Have you been back home yet?

C: 牢记你心中的道德律法，将筑城者的意志贯彻到生活的点点滴滴当中！永远、永远不要辜负伟大的克里珀！
E: Remember the moral law in your heart, and carry out the will of the Architects in each and every part of your life! Never, ever fail the almighty Qlipoth!

C: 请多关照！
E: Great seeing you!

C: 都是朋友嘛，应该的。
E: We're friends, and that's what we do.

C: 要不咱干脆放弃吧…
E: Why don't we just give up...

C: 史瓦罗先生说，它的力量非常强大。不管是谁拥有了它，肩上都会多出很重的担子……
E: Mr. Svarog said that it is super powerful. He said that it will put a very heavy burden on whoever holds it.

C: 地髓开拓团成立于大守护者「智者亚历珊德拉」执政时期。这位守护者资助创办了贝洛伯格学院，借此进行与地下开拓有关的研究。
E: The Geomarrow Development Group was established during the governance of the Supreme Guardian, Sage Alexandra. This Guardian sponsored and founded the Belobog Academy, and later performed Underground trailblazing research.

C: 哦！磐岩镇今天也是和平的一天！
E: Mm-hmm! Another peaceful day in Boulder Town!

C: 希儿，还有我那个毛躁的徒弟，他们一定会继续为自己的家园战斗。只要他们的初心不变，我对下层区的未来就无限看好。哈哈哈！
E: Seele and my rowdy apprentice, they will keep fighting for their home. So long as their aspirations never change, I have nothing but high hopes for the Underworld's future. Hahaha!

C: 你和朗道家族的关系？
E: What's your relationship with the Landau family?

C: 嗯，我是键盘手。
E: Yeah, I'm the keyboard player.

C: 扑满扭动着身子，似乎在等待你的行动。
E: The Trotter is wriggling about. It seems to be waiting for you to make a move.

C: 在你一阵忙活的时候，小熊自顾自地睡着了。
E: As you were wrapped up in all this, the little cub fell asleep.

C: 嗐，快别提了……
E: Ack, don't remind me...

C: 你看这个广告……
E: Check out this ad...

C: 有没有可能……
E: Is it possible...

C: 再等等…奶奶的腰僵住了……
E: Hold on a moment... I got a stiff back...

C: 我来播报今天的新闻……
E: I'm here with the latest in today's news...

C: 再讲一个，再讲一个！
E: Another, another!

C: 我来播报今天的新闻……
E: I'm here with the latest in today's news...

C: 别急，先让我想想……
E: Don't worry. Let me think it over...

C: 好拳！
E: What a punch!

C: 哇，是荣誉队员{NICKNAME}！你在博物馆里有没有看到坏人呀？
E: Wow, it's our honorary member {NICKNAME}! Did you see any bad guys in the museum?

C: 如果想要了解展品背后的故事，尽管来找我——这馆子里的不少展品都是我捐的，要说谁更了解它们，那就必须是我了……
E: If you want to know the story behind the exhibits, feel free to approach me. Many of the exhibits in this museum were donated by me. There is no one more familiar with their stories than I do...

C: 哔嘟…不要把我关进展柜里——我要回星际和平家电门店！我要回星际和平家电门店！
E: Beep beep... Do not lock me back in the display case... Must return to Interastral Peace Appliances! Must return to Interastral Peace Appliances!

C: 我原本设想这样的囚犯在单独的囚室之中应该不会有事，后来不知道他从哪里弄了一柄铁勺，持续不断敲击着囚室墙壁，最终让隔壁囚室囚犯精神崩溃了。
E: I had assumed he wouldn't cause any trouble if he was locked away in a solitary cell. Yet he somehow managed to get his hands on an iron spoon that he endlessly tapped the wall of his cell with, eventually driving the prisoner in his neighboring cell into a mental breakdown.

C: 太卜司不要我，我还可以在这里开家小小的卜算道具商店。化外民老爷们没法在太卜司里求得预言，大可以来到我这里求签算卦嘛。
E: Even if the Divination Commission didn't want me, I could still run my little old divination paraphernalia shop here. Outworlders who can't get their fortunes at the Commission could come to me instead.

C: 说来也不怕您笑话，我们仙舟的厨子们都很忌讳将自己做菜的秘诀公之于众，甚至将一些很普通的技巧神秘化……
E: You might laugh at us, but we Xianzhou chefs are very careful about our recipes being leaked to the public. Some of our most common cooking methods have even been mystified...

C: 那我岂不是血统不纯？
E: Then wouldn't that mean I'm not pure-blooded?

C: 嗤，因为在那里，接入元域是免费的。
E: Pah! Because there, it's free to access the metafield.

C: 怎么想到在幽囚狱做生意？
E: What made you start doing business in The Shackling Prison?

C: 按她的说法：「让你们这些无所事事的囚犯们有点事做，也省得你们惹是生非、打架斗殴。」不过我本来也不喜欢打架啦～
E: As she puts it: "Giving bored prisoners something to do will keep your noses clean and prevent you from starting fights." Of course, I didn't enjoy fighting to begin with.

C: 哎呀，多亏了你们几位英雄，现在金人巷越来越繁荣了，我的生意也越来越好了。
E: Because of you guys, the Aurum Alley has become so much more prosperous, and my business is doing better, too.

C: 我走了。
E: I'm off.

C: 那也太惨了。
E: That's a real shame.

C: 你…你该不会就是近日随云骑军搜捕恶党的……
E: Y—You... You're not the one who's been running around with the Cloud Knights, locking up crooks... right?

C: 神策府是军机重地，实在算不得什么散步的好地方。
E: The Seat of Divine Foresight deals with high-level military secrets. It's really not the kind of place where you could just take a stroll.

C: 我可没有说过，肯定是你记错了。
E: I never said that. You must have misheard me.

C: 其实卑职心里也清楚，神策府的侍卫长并不是什么难以替代的岗位。将军大人不肯放卑职去前线，只是卑职曾经有过魔阴身风险，担心卑职出危险罢了。
E: As a Chief Guard, I know it's not that hard to find my replacement. The general refused to send me to the frontline only because I was at risk of becoming mara-struck, and he was worried about my safety.

C: 唉…可是现在想想那些再也没能回家的将士，卑职也不知道卑职当时到底该不该坚持去前线了……
E: But now, thinking of those warriors who couldn't make it back home, I don't even know if I should've insisted going to the frontline...

C: 但是太岁机巧不是玉兆，玉兆阻塞干扰是没办法对太岁机巧奏效的。
E: But a Lucidum Ingenium isn't a jade abacus, and jade abaci inhibitors have no effect on Lucidum Ingenia.

C: 其实也没那么枯燥。毕竟这儿有堆积如山的档案可以看，随便一本就记录了某个大案要犯的一生，可刺激了！
E: It really isn't that boring at all. After all, there is a mountain of files here to read. I can take a flick through a file that records the entire life of a major criminal as I please. It's pretty thrilling!

C: {NICKNAME}英雄！您又来了～有什么事要办吗？还是想来买个逾期处理的邮包来试试手气？
E: {NICKNAME} the Hero! You're back~ What business brings you here? Or have you come looking to try your luck with an abandoned parcel?

C: 我还有事，先走了。
E: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

C: 不过「带着棺木」听着就很神秘，我以前看过星际和平影视里的一个作品——孤独的枪手总是拖着副棺材漫步在泥泞的荒野上，从匪帮手里救下了一位姑娘。
E: But this "merchant with a coffin" you're asking about sounds very mysterious. I once saw a show on IPM with this solo gunslinger dragging along a coffin as he stalks the muddy wilderness, saving a girl from bandits' clutches.

C: 幽囚狱暴乱那日，狱中一片大乱，一批罪囚企图趁乱窜逃。
E: It was the day of the riot, the entire prison was in complete chaos, and an entire group of prisoners attempted to use the commotion as cover for their escape.

C: 没关系，反正今天我还没开单，现在闲着也是闲着。您要是感兴趣，我可以跟您介绍一下我的业务——我敢说罗浮上，没人比我更精通货物装卸的事项了。
E: No worries. Anyway, I haven't even made a sale yet today and I'm just chilling. If you like, I can tell you a little bit about my work. And I'll bet there's nobody else aboard the Luofu who's better at handling freight than me.

C: 我不打扰了，请继续演奏。
E: I won't bother you any further. Keep on playing.

C: 太卜大人好清静，您也别在这里到处打听了。再见，不送。
E: The master diviner likes her peace and quiet, and you should stop asking so many questions around here. So long for now. Please let yourself out.

C: 你好像对职场氛围颇有微词。
E: You seem to be pretty critical about the workplace culture here.

C: 阴险狡诈，难以靠近。
E: She's sneaky, cunning, and unreliable.

C: 没错，药王秘传依然有残党混迹于此。只要建木和它的传说还在，求药的叛党就会根除不绝，我守在此处也是为了还这里百姓一份安全。
E: Yes. There're still a few Disciples of Sanctus Medicus lurking around here. So long as the Arbor and its legend exist, the Disciples will never truly be eradicated. I'm stationed here to give the few people remaining here a sense of security.

C: 前些日子我收到了军务厅的来信，让我接受测试，检查是否有陷入魔阴身的苗头。不过这又如何呢？我决心还是在这里站好最后一班岗。
E: A few days back, I got a letter from the military affairs office asking me to undergo a test to check if I have any signs of mara. But even if I do, so what? I'm determined to keep on standing here to the bitter end.

C: 您说的倒也没错，说不定持明族也在羡慕我呢。
E: You're right. Maybe the Vidyadhara envy me, too?

C: 我天性如此，并非针对您，客官不必见怪。
E: It's just the way I am. Please don't take any offense. It's nothing personal.

C: 哎，以前我没得选，只能继承家业。
E: *Sigh*... Before, I had no choice but to take on the family business.

C: 生意的确重要，但凡药三分毒，没事啊还是别乱吃药，我也不能瞎开药害人。
E: Business is important, I know that. But every medicine has its side effects and shouldn't just be flogged off indiscriminately. I can't just prescribe potentially harmful drugs to anyone.

C: 哎，他们都说我有卧底经验，让我继续待几年看看。我就知道，迟早会有这么一天的。不知道何时才能再回我云骑军中去……
E: *Sigh* They want me to stay here for a couple more years because I've got undercover experience. I've seen this coming. When will I be able to return to the Cloud Knights...

C: 十王司在此主理公务，无关人等一律回避。
E: The Ten-Lords Commission are handling official matters here. If this does not concern you, please leave.

C: 这些私人物品的「价值」都是人生经历赋予的…所以客人您也不必在意，就大大方方地挑选好了。
E: The "value" of these personal items is conferred by life experiences... So you don't really have to give it much thought. Just pick out anything you like.

C: 保证物美价廉。
E: I guarantee you high quality at a low price.

C: 我怎么可能有事呢，我现在好得不得了。一想到自己正在破解的是全银河最复杂的监狱，我浑身上下都充满了斗志。
E: Why would I not be okay? I've never been better! Just knowing that I am in the process of cracking the most complex prison in the entire cosmos fills my whole body with fighting spirit!

C: 但我这人吧，进了局子就不老实。刚开始他们把我关进地衡司的治安所，那里实在是太无聊了，我没忍住就跑了出来。
E: But I just can't sit still. It was when they locked me up that I started misbehaving. At first, they had me locked up in the Realm-Keeping Commission penitentiary, but I was bored to tears, so I had to escape.

C: 幽囚狱里的奇葩还不够多么，什么可以变成其他囚犯样子的变形怪，能思考会说话的玉兆之类的，我这都算是小场面了。
E: There's enough weird stuff going on in The Shackling Prison. A shapeshifter that can transform into other prisoners, a Jade Abacus that can think and talk... What I do is not a big deal at all in here.

C: 我没什么想问的了。
E: I don't have any more questions.

C: 步离人越狱的时候你在场？
E: Were you here when the borisin escaped?

C: 哈哈哈哈，您是想说「严峻」对吗？那是您多虑了。
E: Hahahaha, do you want to say "harsh"? That's not really the case.

C: 你的评价真苛刻啊。
E: That was pretty harsh.

C: 商户们会接受吗？
E: Will those merchants accept your offer?

C: 这次就不为难你了，不过你最好小心一点，我这人脾气不好，下次就没有这么讲道理了。
E: I won't give you a hard time today, but be careful. I have my moods, and you might just end up on my bad side the next time I see you.

C: 你好像对放生很感兴趣的，你还有什么想要了解的吗？
E: You seem pretty interested in releasing. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

C: 说起来，我也不是第一次来仙舟了。这次来不为别的，只为感受罗浮上最地道的风土人情。
E: This isn't my first trip to Xianzhou. This time, I came only to experience the most authentic Luofu customs and culture.

C: 「六眼飞鱼」的那个鱼翅，可别提多好吃了！那一份鱼翅煲，是咱平生吃过最好吃的……
E: The fins of that fish were delicious beyond compare! That fin stew was the tastiest dish I'd ever had...

C: 不，这就是茶。
E: No, this is tea.

C: 欢迎客人常来啊。
E: My door is always open for you, dear customer.

C: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
E: Has business been going well lately?

C: 让我连线，我现在就要连线。
E: I love planning stuff. I wanna do it now!

C: 小秘书怎么看待星际和平公司？
E: What do you think of the Interastral Peace Corporation?

C: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
E: Has business been going well lately?

C: 我打算继续精进自己的演奏技术，到时请您再多多批评。
E: Looks like I still need to hone my performance skills. Please, do come back next time.

C: 这是一种叫作「编钟」的乐器，据说在仙舟起航之初就已经存在了。
E: This is an ancient musical instrument called "chimes," which existed even as early as when this Xianzhou ship began its voyage.

C: 我一不小心就说太多了，您一时半会儿也很难理解。不过没关系，音乐本就应该是所听即所得，您只需要用心聆听，无需其他准备。
E: Oh, please forgive me for overwhelming you with my own interest. But don't worry about those technical details, because music is meant to be heard, not analyzed.

C: 你一直都有演出吗？
E: Have you been performing all the time?

C: 此象得主意志坚定，勤勉向上。对于你所问之事——此事需投入大量的准备工作，但你的辛勤皆有回报，更上一层楼，楼上风光无限好。切勿自怨自艾，勿要固执己见，切记有耕耘必有收获。
E: The bearer of this sign is determined and diligent. As for the question in your mind — this matter will require a lot of preparation, but your diligence will eventually pay off. Only by reaching the peak can one get the best view. Do not complain about the world, and do not be too strongly opinionated. Have faith in your efforts, because they will bear fruit.

C: 欸？我记得你，「闷葫芦」的朋友，找本姑娘是有什么事吗？
E: Oh, I remember you. You're friends with that "Silent but Deadly," right? What can I do for you?

C: 有什么推荐的话本吗？
E: Do you have any stories to recommend?

C: 我猜想是符太卜让你产生了一些错觉，误以为「将军」是一个人人渴望的职位。实际上倒也并非如此。
E: I believe you've got the wrong impression from Diviner Fu that being the general is everyone's dream. That's not really the case.

C: 医生不见了，不合适吧？
E: Wouldn't that be rather inappropriate for the doctor to run away?

C: 我也想变身成机巧鸟或金人…
E: I want to transform into a cycrane or aurumaton too...

C: 但吾也承认，凭依于金人之中，挥挥手臂便将孽物碾为齑粉…的确快意。
E: But I must also admit that crushing abominations with my own fists as an aurumaton... does feel satisfying.

C: 听见就听见了吧，{NICKNAME}大人不必在此时假装无事发生。
E: It's okay if you heard me, you don't need to pretend that nothing happened, {NICKNAME}.

C: 想问你点事。
E: I'd like to ask you something.

C: 好哦，注意安全。
E: Sure. Be careful.

C: 绥园以前是什么样的？
E: How was Fyxestroll Garden like in the past?

C: 十王司已将此处封锁，请一般市民不要在此处闲逛。
E: This area has been sealed off by the Ten-Lords Commission. Kindly refrain from loitering around in this area.

C: 建木竟然长起来了…
E: I can't believe the Ambrosial Arbor is growing...

C: 怎么样？亲不火速入手一台，在安全距离尽情远观建木盛景吗？
E: What do you think, honey? How about buying one and using it to enjoy the marvelous view of the Ambrosial Arbor from a safe distance?

C: 我笑着问他是不是看了什么讲妖魔当铺的话本故事，误信了小说家言。这爿小店只能以物换钱，或是以钱换物。
E: I laughed and asked him if he'd read some nonsensical story about demon pawnshops and actually believed it. This shop only exchanges items for cash, or vice versa.

C: 我喜欢喝清淡的。
E: I like it mild.

C: 星槎海简直就是罗浮仙舟上的美食洼地，全银河失业的厨师都来这里找到了工作。
E: Starskiff Haven is where the art of cooking goes to die on the Xianzhou Luofu. All the unemployed chefs across the cosmos have found jobs here.

C: 你误会了，我只是个偶尔烧菜的食客罢了，今天是专程来星槎海涨学问来的。
E: You misunderstand. I am but a foodie who sometimes cooks. I have specially come here today to widen my culinary horizons on the Starskiff Haven.

C: 小仙我偷偷地逃出仙舟，在外面的世界生活了几百年，才刚被云骑军给抓回来。
E: I stole away from Xianzhou and lived for a few centuries in the otherworlds. I was recently captured by the Cloud Knights.

C: 我不想伤人，也不敢伤人，就随他去了衙门。他就审我，到底是从哪儿来的什么人。
E: I didn't want to hurt anyone, nor did I dare to, so I followed him to the magistrate's court, where he interrogated me on my background.

C: 我是挺高兴的，但心里隐隐觉得，自己的仙人生涯怕是要到头了。云骑军但凡精细一些，必定能顺着线头抓到我的。唉，果不其然。
E: I was pretty pleased with myself, but I knew it was probably the end of my time as a celestial. If the Cloud Knights were any good at their job, they would follow the trail and capture me. *sigh* And so they did.

C: 我以前见过驭空大人手持弯弓「云鸢铣珧」百步穿杨，那场面真是……
E: I once saw Madam Yukong with her Cloudkite Resplendent Longbow, shooting with tremendous accuracy from a hundred feet away. Truly a sight to—

C: 改造自然的也不是肌肉吧？
E: I doubt nature was reshaped by muscles.

C: 当然了！只要在这条筋肉之道上走得足够远，就能够改变宇宙规则、逆转时空！
E: Absolutely! If we push far enough along the Path of Brawn, we could even rewrite the laws of the universe and reverse time!

C: 仙舟的物流真是方便呐。
E: Logistics on Xianzhou is really something else.

C: 让姐姐考虑一下……
E: Let me consider it...

C: 我将亲自出击。
E: I'll take you on myself.

C: 当然，这也不是全无代价，他们也要付出一点小小的代价——在这个方案里，他们必须牺牲掉自然光…但这都是小问题，他们失去了日光，但是获得了安全。
E: Of course, it would come at the expense of blocking out all sunlight, but that's a small price for safety.

C: 你是谁啊？
E: Who are you?

C: 驭空大人确实非常有魅力，但是她就像是遥远的启明星，我应该远远看着她、欣赏她。一旦我想要接近她，就会觉得遥不可及。
E: She is very charming, but I see her as a star to appreciate from afar. She's beyond my approach.

C: 再次感谢你的帮助！
E: Once again, thank you for your help!

C: 你去把价格打下来，薄利多销。
E: Slash the prices. Go for low profit but high sales.

C: 今天暂时没有想聊的了。
E: I don't have anything to talk about today.

C: 鹤运物流，寄寄寄寄…
E: Heron Express, any-any-any-any-any...

C: 既然来了，那就得寄！
E: Since it's here, I'd better mail it!

C: 去你的吧！
E: Shut your pie hole!

C: 一开始我也是一本正经的追求着报道真实，但数据惨的一塌糊涂。
E: When I first started out, I was a proper journalist who cared about accurate reporting too. Unfortunately, the analytics for my work was absolutely dismal.

C: 「吹捧的通稿」？
E: "Laudatory press releases"?

C: …你不会是抓我回去的吧。
E: ...Have you come to get me?

C: 那后来呢？后来那颗星球怎么样了？
E: Then what? What happened to the planet after that?

C: 刚才真是失礼了。我脑子被工作塞满了，一下子没认出您…不过您就算是罗浮贵客，这引擎舱终归是竞锋舰的核心重地，希望你不要随意操作这里的任何东西。
E: I apologize for my rudeness. My brain's been overloaded by work, and I didn't recognize you immediately... But even if you're a Luofu VIP, the engine room is crucial to the Skysplitter — I really hope you don't touch anything in here.

C: 你说不行，就真的不行吗？
E: When you say, "Don't touch anything," did you really mean it?

C: 没事，咱这也不算是「正统仙舟功夫」，也许你会感兴趣。
E: No worries, this doesn't really count as "traditional Xianzhou martial arts," maybe you'll be interested.

C: 你到长乐天的各大拳馆打听打听，骂得声量最大的，哪个敢和我放开了打？
E: Go ask around the major boxing gyms at the Exalting Sanctum. Those who trash-talk the loudest, who dares to spar with me openly?

C: 唉…你是对的，确实如此。我把他给害死了，这是无可争辩的事实。
E: *Sigh*... You're right. I got him killed, and that's the indisputable truth.

C: 焱攸。
E: Immola.

C: …我也不知道，没有岁阳研究过啊…难道你想说「没有」？
E: ...I don't know either. No heliobi has ever studied it before... Are you implying there is no such thing?

C: 资本主义…我听过这个词。但那不过是你们人类给金钱力量套上的无聊概念之一罢了。
E: Capitalistic... I've heard of that word before. But that's just one of those boring concepts you humans wrap around the power of money.

C: 我们怎么没有说过话了？
E: What do you mean we haven't met before?

C: 你小子还挺狂啊。要不是我被囚禁在这镇妖塔中，我可要对你大打出手了。
E: You talk pretty big, kid. If I weren't imprisoned in this suppression tower, I would have already punched you.

C: 不聊了，再见。
E: Enough talk. Bye.

C: 刚才的招式还不够厉害？
E: Are my moves not impressive enough?

C: 哈哈，亲爱的，我就吃你这款，真是舍不得让你离开了。
E: Haha, my dear, you're just my type. I really can't bear to let you go.

C: 你这样做就不怕被打坏？
E: You're not afraid of being damaged?

C: 没别的事了，告辞。
E: I don't have anything to ask, goodbye.

C: 「多见者博，多闻者智，拒谏者塞，专己者孤」，多读书、多思考、多观察世间万物，学养自然会积少成多。
E: "Seeing Is Learning, Reading Is Growing. Isolation Is Desolation, Self-Full Is Self-Dull." Read more, think more, and observe more, and you'll naturally become knowledgeable along the way.

C: 你听说了吗？建木…
E: Have you heard? The Ambrosial Arbor...

C: 网文太长，动不动连载两百年…
E: Web articles are too long, and they've all got two hundred years of backstory...

C: 答案是三又四分之三…
E: The answer is three and three-quarters...

C: 听说太卜司戒严放假…
E: I heard the Divination Commission is on vacation due to martial law...

C: 鉴定一下热门美食。
E: Time to see what these local delicacies have to offer!

C: 又好吃，又美味。
E: They are delicious.

C: 可他们给的实在太多了。
E: But they offered a price I couldn't refuse...

C: 哎，一言难尽……
E: Alas, it's complicated...

C: 你怎么突然文绉绉的？
E: Why are you waxing poetic all of a sudden?

C: 你误会我了伙计，我说的是咱们这一辈子终极目标。
E: That's not what I'm saying, buddy. I'm talking about our lifelong goals.

C: 发、发生什么了？
E: Wh—What happened?

C: 下次再和你们玩！
E: Let's play again next time!

C: 那时候，山之民豢养的小奇美拉也有着长长的角，是灵活的冲锋选手，每逢开战时，大地兽在前面踏起滚滚尘土，遮蔽敌人的视线……
E: Back then, the little chimeras raised by the Mountain Dwellers also had long horns and were agile chargers. During combat, the dromases would kick up clouds of dust at the front, obscuring the enemy's vision...

C: 说起来，自担任哀地里亚的督战圣女以来，我大部分时候都是自己一人编发的…毕竟想要麻烦别人替我梳理头发，并不是那么容易。
E: Come to think of it, ever since I became Aidonia's Maiden of War, I'd been styling my own hair most of the time... After all, it's not easy to trouble others to help me with that.

C: 好好享受这一刻吧。
E: Savor this moment.

C: 当然有——我的「后」棋。
E: Of course there is. My "Queen" piece.

C: 我先准备准备…
E: Let me get ready first...

C: 真希望平安退休啊。
E: I just want a peaceful retirement.

C: 袭击狱卒者重罚…
E: Assaulting prison guards is a serious offense...

C: 之所以选择园艺盆栽，是因为其类山水，有种以景抒怀的感觉，能让我想到在狱外的时光。
E: I chose to grow potted plants because they allow me to express myself, reminding me of my time outside of the prison.

C: 来诃教授，我是负责查案的诗琳，我能向你请教吗？
E: Professor Laihe, I'm Shilin, the one who is in charge of this case. Can I ask you a few questions?

C: 剑术只有高低之分，修行时间并不能决定一切。
E: The only thing that matters in swordplay is skill. The time spent training doesn't mean much at the end.

C: 呃…要不要看看我们特等奖：限定战损版无罅飞光，错过可就再也没有了！
E: Um... Would you like to take a look at our special prize, the limited Transcendent Flash Wounded version? If you miss it, you won't find this bargain elsewhere!

C: 别忘了，深渊也在凝视你。
E: Alright, don't forget that the abyss stares back at you.

C: *比较刺耳的通用粗口*
E: *Crude common vulgarities*

C: 很遗憾，没有。你从令人眩晕的夹缝中逃得一命，获得了有一个有用的冷知识。
E: Unfortunately, no. You narrowly escaped from the dizzying gap, gaining an unexpectedly useful piece of trivia.

C: 魔术师。
E: A magician.

C: 滚筒洗衣机。
E: A cylindrical washing machine.

C: 我是～滚筒洗衣机！
E: I am~ A cylindrical washing machine!

C: 不要让算法取代你的选择
E: Do not allow the algorithm to make your choices for you

C: 好了，现在请睁开眼睛。
E: Okay, please open your eyes.

C: 战术性撤退也是一种智慧，此时的退避也是为了下一次的胜利。
E: Tactical retreat is also wise. This retreat is also for the next victory.

C: 你想起那些飞在空中运送货物的机巧鸟——若是它们送货时不巧爪子一滑，若是有行人不巧经过它们的下方…你摇了摇头，不敢再想下去。
E: The image of cycranes that deliver goods in the air pops up in your mind. What if the goods slip off their claws when they are flying? What if a pedestrian happens to pass by below them? You shake your head, dreading to continue the train of thought.

C: 很可能是一只饭盒，也可能装着饼干等零食，还可能装满了某人祖母的针线。
E: It might be a lunchbox or a box that contains snacks like cookies or a sewing kit of someone's grandmother.

C: 嗯？谁？我应该认识吗？
E: Eh? Who? Should I know you?

C: 科技与武术的结合！
E: The combination of technology and martial arts!

C: 别太明显了，过来点。
E: Don't be so obvious, come closer.

C: 我们不能输！
E: We can't lose!

C: 前卫但素雅的园林造景，抚慰了无数过劳观星士的疲惫身心。
E: The modern yet elegant landscape has soothed the tired souls of countless overworked stargazers.

C: 据说「织机」不该有这么庞大，只是设计师看错了图纸——但这也许只是谣言罢了。
E: It is said that a loom isn't supposed to be so large, but the designer has misread the drawings. Although it could be just a rumor.

C: 检索「空间」的阵基，可以从寰宇诸界已知的全部地理信息中筛选出与检索对象相关的经纬。
E: This is a base terminal of space. It can filter the coordinates relating to your target by searching from all the known geographical information in the universe.

C: 「内有金人守卫——请勿擅闯，后果自负。」
E: "This place is guarded by the aurumatons. Keep off at your peril."

C: 司辰宫员工会在下班后利用沙盘进行战棋游戏。驭空是其中一等一的高手，总能让其他员工失去游戏体验。
E: The workers at the Palace of Astrum use the sandpit to play warchess after work, and Yukong is the most excellent player among them. The game will lose all its fun when your opponent is Yukong.

C: 音乐使我升华！
E: Music elevates me!

C: 「文学部」了解一下？
E: Interested in the Literature Club?

C: 你可能得好好考虑一下，自己从出生至今已欠下多少命债了。
E: You may have to think hard about how all the life debt you've racked up since you were born.

C: 你将蜷缩的纸团展开，却见其中的话语简直称得上是触目惊心——
E: You unfold the curled-up paper only to find a piece of devastating news...

C: 这页的内容似乎与评书有关。
E: The content of this page seems to be related to storytelling.

C: 这一页介绍了持明时调。
E: This page introduces Vidyadhara Elegies.

C: 那孽物重重坠地，你勉力定住惊魂，才能仔细打量：它半似龙首的头颅上，有长髯与兽毛飘扬。你直直看向它水银般的眼眸，惊觉其中有一丝惶恐。
E: The abomination falls to the ground with a loud thud. You try your best to steady yourself before examining the abomination. Its semi-draconic head bears long whiskers and flowing beastly fur. You stare directly into its mercury-like eyes, sensing a hint of fear within them.

C: 它想要烈火，想要热砂，想要锻炉的高温和锤砧的歌唱。它向你断断续续讲述云骑军器的研造之法，向你反复低诵辨认矿石的歌诀。
E: It yearns for ferocious fire and burning sand. It desires the heat of the forge and the singing of the anvil. Intermittently, it tells you about the research and production method of Cloud Knight weapons and repeatedly recites a song about how to recognize ores.

C: 这颗晶莹剔透的持明卵中有奇光游移，吸引着你的目光。
E: The glimmer of the crystal-clear Vidyadhara egg catches your eye.

C: 你抬手敲了敲卵壳，里面传来了一阵奇异的味道。你仔细分辨，感觉到里面似乎夹杂着泥土的芬芳、高温油炸的香气、铁锈味…种种味道混杂，实在难以言喻。
E: You raise your hand and tap on the eggshell, and a weird smell emanates from inside. You evaluate the smell and detect the scent of soil, frying oil, and rusted iron... It is hard to describe the smell as it is a mixture of various scents.

C: 这副广告画看起来似乎是在描绘「进餐前拍照」这种古老神圣的仪式，但也不禁让人担心右下角的那位朋友是否吃上了美食。
E: This ad seems to show the ancient, sacred ritual of "taking a picture of the food before one eats it." However, one can't help but worry whether the person in the bottom right got to eat any food.

C: 事关重大，立刻拆开！
E: The matter at hand is grave! Time to tear it open immediately!

C: 难道他来演给我们看？
E: Is he going to perform for us?

C: 不管这对恋人间发生了什么样的龃龉，时将一只小狗装在狭窄逼仄的快递箱中邮来寄去，这绝不为任何有爱心的无名客所乐见。
E: No matter what happened between the lovers, mailing a puppy back and forth in a cramped little delivery box would upset any caring Nameless.

C: 按照「信仰质量理论」，即使三者质量相同，「帝弓遗物」对仙舟民来说也明显更重一些。
E: According to the "faith mass theory," even if the three items are of the same mass, Xianzhou citizens would perceive the Reignbow's Relic to be significantly heavier.

C: 这世上怎么没有鬼？
E: Why wouldn't there be ghosts?

C: 那你可以尝尝失恋。
E: Well, you can try the dumped ones.

C: 你本想去后台再探究竟，此时你通过了一次「历史」检定——戏台上的门不应该是这样的，应该是「出将」和「入相」，那么这里究竟是…？
E: You originally wanted to explore the backstage further, but at this moment, you pass a History check — doors onstage shouldn't look like this. Shouldn't they be labeled "dextral entrance" and "sinistral exit"? Then what is this place...?

C: 电话挂断的声音传来，随后是身体栽倒在沙发上的声音。
E: You hear the sound of the phone being hung up, followed by someone slumping down onto a sofa.

C: 高脚杯中没有一滴酒，全是苏乐达。
E: There's not a drop of alcohol in the goblet. It's all SoulGlad.

C: 你很好奇，吊灯浮在那个位置究竟是为了照亮什么？
E: You're curious: What exactly is the chandelier illuminating in that position?

C: 你明白，你和她都有要完成的使命，或在梦境、或在现实。为此你们不得不暂时分离，走向命运为各自安排的道路。
E: You know that both of you have a mission to complete, be it in the Dreamscape or reality. To this end, you two are forced to go separate paths, toward your own pre-ordained destinies.

C: 「我们得从这里离开！■■就要打进来了！」
E: "We have to get out of here! ■■ is about to breach this place!"

C: 杯中空无一物，不知何日才能再度填满。
E: The cup is empty, not knowing which day it will be full again.

C: 不过无需紧张，我并不觉得你出现在这里有何不妥。你是一个善良的人，尽管有时会鲁莽行事，打破规则…但那都影响不到你的本性。
E: There's no need to worry. I don't find your presence here inappropriate. You're a kind soul. Although you sometimes act recklessly and tend to break rules, none of those changes your true nature.

C: 听上去不太正规…
E: Sounds fishy to me...

C: 当然，这只是我的浅薄己见，无需放在心上。梦的美妙之处正在于此——它是自由的，没人能阻止你追寻埋藏于梦中的宝藏。
E: Of course, this is just my humble opinion, which you aren't obligated to heed. That's the beauty of a dream — you're free here, and no one can stop you from pursuing the treasures buried within.

C: 可当我真正见到她…却只觉星空寂灭、海洋枯涸。和她相处的每分每秒都显得如此漫长，如此煎熬……
E: But when I see her in person... I feel as though the stars have stopped shining, and the ocean has dried up. Every minute I spend with her feels so long and torturous...

C: 下次还要来和我分享证书的快乐啊。
E: Don't forget to return and share more fun with certificates with me.

C: 为什么会这么空虚，感觉就像…在喝一瓶完全没有汽的苏乐达。
E: Why does it feel so empty, like... drinking an uncarbonated SoulGlad.

C: 逐梦客的类别五花八门，只有一点共通之处——他们全都擅长编织谎言。但我在您的身上嗅不到伪装的味道。
E: Those who chase dreams come from all walks of life, but they all have one thing in common — their deftness in spinning lies. But I do not sense even a tinge of hypocrisy on you.

C: 尽管悲哀的模样因人而异，但像这样会疯狂在速记本上写稿的…你还是第一次见。
E: While the look of tragedy varies among people, this is the first person you've met who writes on the notepad as if they're demon-possessed...

C: 呼…呼呼……
E: Zzz... Zzz...

C: 确认你没有其余意图后，眼前的皮皮西人瞬间跑没影了。
E: After confirming you have no ulterior intentions, the Pepeshi person vanishes in an instant.

C: 我当然知道这是必要的，是维系梦境稳定的手段，但……
E: I know all of this is necessary and a means to maintaining stability in the dreamscape. However...

C: …你听不出它的妙处，是吧？
E: ...You can't hear the splendor of it, can you?

C: 我的愤怒，突然消失了…即便如此，你也必须赔偿我的损失。
E: My anger has vanished all of a sudden... Even so, you should still compensate me for my loss.

C: …这个脆筒我训了好久，怎么也值个一亿信用点吧？
E: ...I've trained this cone for a long time. Shouldn't you compensate me a <unbreak>100</unbreak> million credits?

C: 它们就像我的…伙伴？唉，越说越可悲……
E: They are like my... partners? Boy, now I sound so pathetic...

C: 你母亲是什么样的人？
E: What kind of person is your mom?

C: 阴云重新覆盖了眼前的男人。他目光瞥向不远处的角落，仿佛在寻找可供躲藏的空间。
E: Dark clouds loom over the man before you. He glanced over to a corner not far away, as if looking for a space to hide.

C: 不对，它、它从来都不是我的父亲！它怎么会是我的父亲……
E: No. I—It's never my dad to begin with! How can he be my dad...

C: 我们根据遗嘱取用了他身体中的零件，重新拼合成了响彻整个星系的「哈利伯顿刻心自鸣钟」——
E: As instructed in his will, we collected the parts of his body and reassembled them into the "Haliburton Memorial Chime Clock" that resounds throughout the entire galaxy...

C: 我第一次在钟表上听到如此轻盈的计时声，像是加多了机油，轴承的转动变得顺滑又灵敏……
E: This is the first time I've heard such a light chiming on a clock. The bearings are rotating smoothly and flexibly as if I've added too much engine oil...

C: 你是交通志愿者吗？
E: Are you a traffic volunteer?

C: 冷静不下来的。
E: The one who can't calm down.

C: 他那幅孱弱的身板，哪还像个猎犬的模样？要是被家族里的长辈看到了，不笑掉大牙才怪。
E: Just look at that puny body of his — how could he resemble anything close to a Bloodhound? If The Family old-timers were to see him, they'd laugh themselves to their graves.

C: 嗨呀，我说的就是那些筑梦师啊，这帮人可好玩了——
E: No, I'm talking about those Dreamweavers. They're a fun lot...

C: 那敢情好啊，我正愁在这边找不到人聊天呢！
E: That would be fantastic. I've been hankering for a chat around here!

C: 呃…嗐——这不是好不容易来一次梦里嘛，自然要和平日里做出些区别。
E: Err... Well, it's a rare treat to be in a dream — gotta do something that's a little different from your humdrum life.

C: 你不是在和他们聊天吗？
E: Aren't you chatting with them?

C: 来聊聊天吧。
E: Let's chat.

C: 绝赞！我就这么说吧，如果不是生存所迫，谁不想一辈子待在梦里？！
E: Super awesome! Let me put it this way, if it weren't a matter of survival, I don't think anyone would ever want to leave this dream!

C: 我其实有些摇摆…艺术创作是否应该有所谓的规范呢？
E: I'm actually starting to waver... Is artistic creation even supposed to have these so-called standards?

C: 我以前是卡戎•塔尔金星系联盟的建筑系教授，现在是折纸大学筑梦技术系的教授。
E: I was once an architecture professor in the Charon-Targin System Alliance. Now I am a professor of dreamweaving technology at Paperfold University.

C: 我其实每天都想从折纸大学辞职。梦境里的人比外面的人还务虚，有时候我都想一人赏他们一闷棍，再拖到小黑屋里打到他们放弃梦想为止。
E: If I'm honest, I think about quitting my job at Paperfold University every day. People in the dreamscape are even more unrealistic than those outside. Sometimes, I want to beat them for their ideas, then drag them into a dark room and beat them until they abandon their dreams entirely.

C: 祝您在匹诺康尼度过一段愉快的时光。
E: We wish you a pleasant stay in Penacony.

C: 你听到了演奏者的咕哝声。
E: You notice faint hums escaping the pianist as he plays.

C: 我瞧您还是生面孔…刚刚才来到匹诺康尼吗？
E: I see you are not quite accustomed to this place... Are you new to Penacony?

C: 随时恭候您的光临。
E: Looking forward to seeing you next time.

C: 根据字面意义，入梦即进入梦境。
E: Well, "dream" refers to the process of entering the Dreamscape.

C: 嗯？这论坛注册怎么还要答题的…你知道吗，机动球竞赛的犯规动作？
E: Hmm? I need to answer questions before signing up for this forum?... "Do you know about the foul play in Roboball?"

C: 如果您与其他宾客或是酒店工作人员发生纠纷，可以随时呼叫我。
E: If you have any disputes with other guests or hotel staff, please feel free to contact me at any time.

C: 之前我们粘土人必须依靠公司才能完成星际旅行，每次光是甲板的清洁费就要花掉不少信用点。
E: We Clayforms needed IPC services for interstellar travel, and it cost us a significant amount of credits just to clean the decks.

C: 我宣布，本届「星际决胜庆典」的冠军首席是——银狼！
E: I hereby declare... the champion of this year's Interastral Tournament Festival... is Silver Wolf!

C: 一台设计十分荒诞的电话机被固定在这里，等待路过的好奇宾客拿起它的听筒。
E: A peculiarly designed telephone is fixed in place, waiting for curious passersby to pick up its receiver.

C: …第三名…桂乃芬，<unbreak>94</unbreak>分。第二名…罗刹，<unbreak>96</unbreak>分。第一名…素裳，<unbreak>99</unbreak>分！
E: 3rd place... Guinaifen, <unbreak>94</unbreak> points. 2nd place... Luocha, <unbreak>96</unbreak> points. And the winner is... Sushang, <unbreak>99</unbreak> points!

C: 叮铃…叮铃…叮铃……
E: *ring*... *ring*... *ring*...

C: 晚上好，尊贵的客人！您今天是否有兴趣打一通神奇的「窥梦电话」？
E: Good evening, esteemed guest! Would you be interested in making a Dreampeek Call today?

C: 说得太好了，一看你就是苏乐达的忠实粉丝！拿去吧，我们一向对老客户宠爱有加。
E: Well said! You're clearly a loyal fan of SoulGlad! Here's your SoulGlad. We've always treated our loyal customers well.

C: 哦~听起来是很酷炫的武器呢！
E: I see... Sounds cool!

C: 据说这种食物如今仍在售卖，只是店家去除了其中的鲱鱼成分。
E: You can still find this product on the shelf now, but herring has been removed from the recipe.

C: 购买方式可登录飞车官网进行查询，请勿轻信市面上的仿制产品，以免出现任何意外。感谢您的配合，祝您生活愉快。
E: You may purchase it on our official website. Please avoid using counterfeit products on the market to prevent any mishaps. Thank you for your cooperation.

C: 给、给你这个，求求你放过我……
E: Th—This is for you. Please let me go...

C: 等等，我还有问题！
E: Hold on. I have some questions!

C: 滴答，需要解释的就这么多啦！记住，<color=#dbc291ff>多多探索美梦小镇、多多赚取金表钞、然后多多把它们带到我的雕像这儿来！</color>下次见咯，灰色的朋友！
E: Tick-tock, that's all I want to say! Remember to <color=#dbc291ff>explore the Dreamville as much as you can, earn Clock Credits, and take them to my statue here!</color> See you, my gray friend!

C: 这样的事情我已见过许多。比起悲伤，我心中更多的是…疲倦。
E: I've seen many such incidents. Compared to sorrow, my heart is more... tired.

C: 被捕之后，他对自己的所作所为全无悔意，反倒咒骂我是背信弃义之人。
E: After his capture, he was remorseless about his vile act, and even cursed me for being someone who had forsaken my professional oath.

C: 仔细想想，之前的我真是太死板了。这样惬意的日子，就该笑着过才对！
E: Come to think of it, I've been too rigid all this while. I should be enjoying such a wonderful life with a smile!

C: 或许是时候改掉这副冷脸了…从现在起，我要热情接纳身边的每一个人——就从你开始吧。
E: Perhaps, it's time I get rid of this poker face... From now on, I must treat everyone around me with warmth. Let's start with you.

C: 突如其来的光芒笼罩了一切。火焰冲上天空，将这副美景烧成灰烬。
E: Suddenly, everything was enveloped by a glaring light. Flames shot up toward the sky, turning this beautiful scene into ashes.

C: 既然家族不愿用强硬的手段惩罚坏蛋，就由我来代为教训他们好了！
E: Since The Family doesn't want to be harsh on the bad guys, I'll do the job!

C: 想要和我一起逛逛美梦小镇吗，滴答！
E: Do you want to walk around Dreamville with me, tick-tock!

C: 我还有很多其他好朋友，他们都是很好的人…有机会我再向你好好介绍他们吧？
E: I have lots of other good friends, too! They are all wonderful people... I'll introduce you to them all when I get an opportunity, okay?

C: 这个吧台就是最好的聊天场所，你想知道些什么呢？
E: This lounge is the perfect place for a chat. What do you want to know?

C: 在常人看来这是一组平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，但他们其实各有所指。
E: To a regular person, they seem to be nothing more than a group of unremarkable Nightingale Statues, but they each have their own meaning.

C: …说好的「书本和知识都是可以共享的」呢？
E: ...I thought "books and knowledge were things that can be shared?"

C: 你发现自己并不太能欣赏房间主人的高雅品味。
E: You realize that you are just not able to appreciate the acquired taste of the chamber's master.

C: 行，这里的音乐也正适合畅谈，你想聊什么话题？
E: Alright, the music here is perfect for a conversation. What would you like to discuss?

C: 我会祝福你们的。
E: I wish you both well.

C: 我会找人把这里的见闻写成报道，让更多观众都知道的，等着上电视吧。
E: I will send someone to write a report on what I've seen here and let our viewers know. Keep an eye on the TV!

C: 想听听你对我的看法。
E: I want to know what you think about me.

C: 神主吸收了太一的「秩序」，相对的，那位陨落的星神也在我等谐乐中留下了杂音。就像一种跨越时空的诅咒，久久盘桓在家族头上。
E: The Great One absorbed Ena of Order. Consequently, that fallen Aeon left behind a dissonance in our harmonious music. It is like some kind of curse that transcends time and space and has hung over The Family's head for ages.

C: 乌云压顶般的阴霾骤然将欧利马的双瞳笼罩，她试图压抑身体的颤抖，但并不成功。
E: Olma's eyes cloud over with a sudden gloominess. She tries to suppress her body's trembling to no avail.

C: 你…我是说我渴望着一片能够自由创造艺术的天地，但那并不存在于星海，也不会存在于梦中！
E: You... I meant that I yearn for a haven where I can freely create art, but it won't exist in the stars, nor does it in dreams!

C: 这里全都是庸俗的家伙——恕我冒昧，你也一样！
E: These are all riffraff here — pardon me if I may say so, but so are you!

C: 脱口秀？！不，这可是为了「谐乐大典」而生的谐乐，不是那种，呃…不太严肃的艺术表现形式。
E: Stand-up comedy? No, this is just a piece for the Charmony Festival. Not like that, er... less serious form of art.

C: 我只是热砂分会场的灯光控制员，平时的工作就是操作这些设备，给现场炒热气氛，致谢名单里都不会出现我的名字，所以才编造了那些经历。
E: I'm just the lighting controller at Scorchsand Hall. My work involves operating this equipment and warming up the crowd. You'd never find my name in the credits of anything, so I made all of those events up.

C: 再这样下去，节目的口碑非得给他们败完了！
E: If I keep this up, the ceremony's reputation is going to be ruined by them!

C: 啊？没什么可分享的吗？好吧……
E: Oh? You've got nothing to share with me? Okay then...

C: 在她温柔的哼唱下，熟悉的旋律仿佛蒙上了不一样的色彩。她注意到你的到来，微笑着向你点头致意。
E: Under the gentle tone of her humming, this familiar melody seems to take on a whole new meaning. As she notices your arrival, she smiles and nods her head to greet you.

C: 在现实中，这些孩子往往都身负某些缺陷，他们或不善与人交往，或自小患有某种疾病…总之，他们是生活在社会暗面的边缘人。
E: In reality, all of these children suffer with some kind of disadvantage. Some of them struggle with social interaction, some of them have struggled with their health from a young age... Whatever it is, they are all marginalized people that live in the shadows of society.

C: 自从在球笼事故中失去行动能力之后，我明白了一件事——相比于怜悯和同情，他们更需要的是尊重和平等对待。
